items.title: The Bastion of Fireblood - Locations
items.content:
The Bastion of Fireblood — Locations

---

Overview

Info
The Land of Burning Hearts
The Bastion of Fireblood is where passion becomes geography. Built from the Left Heart of Aedwynn — the Engine of Passion — this territory burns with emotional fire. Love, hate, jealousy, devotion — every feeling has weight here, and strong emotions can literally ignite.

Organ Base: The Left Heart (Engine of Passion) Law: Feel deeply. Love completely. Burn for what you believe.

---

Geography Overview

The Bastion of Fireblood covers approximately 420,000 square miles — roughly the size of Colombia. It is characterized by:

  • Volcanic highlands: Mountains that burn with emotional rather than geological fire
  • The Heartlands: Fertile valleys fed by rivers of molten passion
  • Ember Fields: Plains where strong feelings cause spontaneous combustion
  • The Passion Deeps: Underground chambers where the Heart still beats
  • Jealousy Wastes: Regions scorched by unrequited love and obsessive hate
The climate is perpetually warm — not from the sun but from the emotional heat radiating from the land itself. Temperatures spike during festivals, battles, or any event that stirs strong feeling.

---

Major Regions

The Crimson Heartlands

Type: Central territory Character: The seat of Kaedris's power; the Heart's surface

The literal surface of the divine heart, now overgrown with civilization. The ground pulses. Rivers of liquid passion (not quite lava, not quite blood) flow through channels. Cities are built around the major arteries, drawing warmth and power from the eternal heartbeat below.

The Passion Peaks

Type: Mountain range Character: Volcanic; pilgrimage sites; emotional weather

Mountains formed from the accumulated passion of millennia — solidified emotions compressed into stone. They erupt not with lava but with concentrated feeling. A Passion Peak eruption can cause mass hysteria, city-wide love affairs, or collective rage in surrounding settlements.

The Ember Fields

Type: Plains Character: Agricultural; dangerous; beautiful

Vast grasslands where the vegetation has adapted to emotional combustion. Crops here grow fast, die fast, and catch fire whenever someone feels too strongly. Farmers learn emotional discipline. Those who can't control themselves aren't farmers for long.

The Jealousy Wastes

Type: Badlands Character: Scorched; haunted; avoided

Where love went wrong. The Jealousy Wastes are regions destroyed by obsessive passion — jealousy, possessive love, unrequited devotion that burned so hot it scarred the land. Nothing grows here. The echoes of old passions still linger, attacking anyone who enters.

---

Key Locations

1. Heartsgate — The Crimson Capital

Type: Capital City Population: ~380,000 Location: Built around the main arterial chamber of the Heart

"Love is not optional here. You will love something — the city, the Matron, your work, your neighbor. And you will burn for it."

Description

Heartsgate is built where the major arteries of the divine heart converge — a city of red stone, warm air, and perpetual twilight glow from the molten passion flowing beneath. The architecture follows the organic curves of the Heart itself, with buildings that seem to grow from the cardiac tissue.

The city is beautiful and terrifying. Public displays of emotion are common, encouraged, mandatory. Apathy is the only crime worse than betrayal. Citizens who lose their passion are... treated.

Districts

DistrictCharacter
The Arterial WayMain thoroughfare; commerce; passion markets
The Devotion QuarterTemples, shrines, romantic establishments
The Ember HeightsWealthy district; cooler (less emotional fire risk)
The Forge WardIndustry; passion-fired metalwork
The Cooling HousesWhere those too passionate are calmed
The Matron's ChamberKaedris's palace; built in the heart's core

Notable Locations

LocationDescription
The Eternal FlameCentral plaza; fire that's burned since the Usurpation
The Passion MarketsTrade in emotional goods, love potions, rage-weapons
The Devotion TemplesWhere citizens renew their commitment to feeling
The Cooling HousesTreatment centers for dangerous emotional excess
The Jealousy PitPrison for those whose love turned toxic
The Matron's EmbraceKaedris's throne; direct access to the Heart
---

2. The Passion Peaks — Volcanic Sacred Sites

Type: Mountain Range / Pilgrimage Sites Population: ~50,000 (dispersed across monasteries and villages) Character: Emotional volcanoes; wisdom through intensity

"The mountains don't erupt with fire. They erupt with feeling. Stand in the ash of a rage-eruption and understand what anger truly means."

Description

A chain of mountains that are volcanoes in a spiritual rather than geological sense. Each peak has a dominant emotional resonance — the Fury Mount, the Sorrow Spire, the Joy Peak, the Desire Caldera. They erupt unpredictably, showering the surrounding area with concentrated emotion.

Pilgrims journey here to experience pure feeling. Monasteries on the slopes teach emotional mastery — not suppression, but intensity without destruction. The monks who live here have felt everything there is to feel and learned to channel rather than be consumed.

Major Peaks

PeakEmotionEffect of Eruption
Fury MountRageMass aggression; violence; warfare
Sorrow SpireGriefDepression; tears; memorial
Joy PeakHappinessCelebration; manic energy; creativity
Desire CalderaLust/WantObsession; pursuit; consumption
Devotion CrownLoveBonding; loyalty; sacrifice
---

3. Ember's Rest — The Farming Heart

Type: Agricultural Hub Population: ~120,000 Location: Central Ember Fields; unusually stable emotional zone

"We grow passion-wheat. It burns if you feel too strongly while harvesting. Farming teaches you control or it teaches you burns."

Description

The largest agricultural settlement in the Bastion, built in an unusually emotionally stable region of the Ember Fields. Here, farmers have developed techniques for growing crops despite the ever-present risk of passion fire.

The people of Ember's Rest are the most emotionally disciplined in the Bastion — not because they feel less, but because they've learned to feel without ignition. This makes them valuable as diplomats, negotiators, and spies in other territories.

Features

FeatureDescription
The Cool FieldsFarmland with dampening wards
Ember AcademySchool for emotional discipline
The FirebreakRing of bare earth around the settlement
Harvest TemplesWhere farmers meditate before work
The Burning BarnsWhere accidents happen; rebuilt constantly
---

4. The Jealousy Wastes — Scorched Lands

Type: Wasteland / Dangerous Region Population: ~5,000 (scattered survivors, exiles, monsters) Character: Love gone wrong; emotional radiation

"My grandmother burned for a man who didn't love her back. Her passion scorched a hundred miles. That's the Wastes now."

Description

Not all passion is healthy. The Jealousy Wastes are regions destroyed by toxic emotion — possessive love, obsessive hate, jealousy that burned so hot it scarred reality itself. The land is blackened, cracked, still warm from fires that burned centuries ago.

Those who enter feel the echoes. Old jealousies resurface. Forgotten hatreds reignite. The Wastes have a way of finding your worst impulses and feeding them until you burn too.

Hazards

  • Jealousy Echoes: Remnant emotions that attach to travelers
  • Passion Wraiths: Ghosts of those who burned themselves out
  • Spontaneous Ignition: Entering with strong feelings causes combustion
  • The Obsession Zones: Areas where you can't stop thinking about someone
  • Emotional Radiation: Prolonged exposure warps personality

Why Go Here?

  • Exile destination for emotional criminals
  • Rare materials (passion-fused minerals)
  • Hidden knowledge (things burned but not destroyed)
  • Penance pilgrimage (suffer your feelings fully)
---

5. The Devotion Temples — Religious Complex

Type: Temple Complex Population: ~30,000 (priests, devotees, pilgrims) Location: Ring of temples surrounding the Matron's Chamber

"Kaedris does not demand worship. She demands love. There is a difference."

Description

A vast complex of temples, shrines, and sacred spaces dedicated to the Crimson Matron and the concept of passionate devotion. Unlike cold worship, devotion here is active, physical, emotional. Services involve dancing, singing, crying, making love, fighting — anything that channels strong feeling toward the divine.

The temples also serve as training grounds for Passion Priests — clergy who can channel emotional fire without being consumed. These priests are invaluable in battle, in healing, and in the... resolution of emotional conflicts.

Major Temples

TempleAspectPurpose
The EmbraceRomantic loveWeddings, bonding ceremonies, sexual rites
The Fury ShrineRighteous angerJustice, vengeance, war blessing
The Grief ChapelLossFunerals, mourning, letting go
The Joy CathedralCelebrationFestivals, births, victories
The Devotion CoreLove for KaedrisDirect worship; communion with Matron
---

6. The Forge of Hearts — Industrial Complex

Type: Manufacturing Center Population: ~80,000 Location: Eastern Heartlands; major arterial junction

"We forge with passion fire. No bellows, no coal — just pure emotion focused into heat. Our steel holds feeling."

Description

The Bastion's industrial center, where passion fire is channeled into creation. The Forge of Hearts produces weapons, armor, jewelry, and tools imbued with emotional properties — swords that burn with rage, armor that strengthens with devotion, jewelry that binds lovers together.

Workers here are carefully selected for emotional intensity and control. A blacksmith who loses focus doesn't just ruin a piece — they potentially incinerate the workshop.

Products

ProductEffect
Rage SteelWeapons that deal extra damage when wielder is angry
Devotion ArmorProtection scales with wearer's conviction
Bond RingsConnected pairs; each knows when the other is in danger
Grief GlassStores tears; releases sorrow when broken
Joy BellsRing to induce happiness in hearers
---

7. The Cooling Houses — Treatment Centers

Type: Medical/Correctional Facility Population: ~15,000 (patients, staff) Location: Throughout the Bastion; largest in Heartsgate

"Some burn too hot. We don't extinguish them — that would be cruel. We teach them to bank their fire."

Description

Not everyone can handle the emotional intensity of the Bastion. Some burn too hot, too fast, endangering themselves and others. The Cooling Houses are treatment centers where the emotionally dangerous are taught control, calmed, and eventually reintegrated.

Treatment is not cruel — the Bastion values passion, not suffering. But it is intensive. Patients undergo meditation, physical exhaustion, emotional confrontation, and magical dampening until they can feel without igniting.

Methods

  • Exhaustion therapy: Physical activity until emotions stabilize
  • Confrontation circles: Face your feelings with professional support
  • Dampening wards: Magical suppression (temporary)
  • Passion channeling: Direct emotion into safe activities
  • The Final Cooling: For those who can't be saved; not discussed
---

8. The First Flame — Sacred Pilgrimage Site

Type: Monument / Pilgrimage Destination Location: The exact center of the Heart's surface

"This flame has burned since the moment Kaedris ripped the Heart from the Maker's chest. It will burn until the world ends."

Description

A fire that has never gone out — the first passion flame, ignited in the moment of the Usurpation. It burns in a plaza at the exact center of the Heart, tended by priests who have served for generations.

Pilgrims come to add their passion to the flame. Lovers throw tokens in. Warriors heat their blades. The grieving burn letters to the dead. Everything offered to the First Flame becomes part of its eternal burning.

Rituals

RitualPurposeOffering
BindingSeal a commitmentToken from both parties
BlessingEmpower an itemThe item itself (returns enhanced)
ReleaseLet go of a feelingWritten expression of the emotion
PetitionAsk Kaedris directlyBlood from the heart (metaphorically)
---

Society & Culture

The Passion Hierarchy

ClassRoleNotes
The Crimson MatronSovereignKaedris herself; embodiment of passion
The DevotedInner circleThose who love Kaedris directly, romantically
Passion PriestsClergyChannel emotion without burning
The ForgedWarriors/ArtisansThose who create with passion fire
CitizensGeneral populationThose who feel, love, hate, live
The CoolingPatientsThose learning control
The ColdOutcastsThose who cannot or will not feel

Emotional Law

Crimes in the Bastion are emotional rather than primarily physical:

CrimePunishment
ApathyForced emotional awakening; exposure to passion
False feelingHumiliation; exile
Jealousy without loveWastes exile
Love betrayedMarked; everyone knows
Passion murder (killing in emotional excess)Cooling House
Cold murder (killing without feeling)Execution; worst crime

The Burning

Strong emotions cause literal fire. This is:

  • Normal and expected
  • Managed through training
  • Channeled into industry
  • Beautiful when controlled
  • Deadly when not
Citizens learn emotional awareness from childhood. Knowing when you're about to ignite and stepping onto fireproof ground is basic survival.

---

Creatures of the Bastion

Native Fauna

CreatureDescription
Passion MothAttracted to strong emotion; beautiful; swarms before eruptions
Ember FoxCanine that runs hot; loyal pets; burn when angry
Heart BeetleFeeds on emotional residue; used for cleaning
Fury BirdRaptor that attacks when it senses anger
Devotion HoundBonds permanently to one person; dies when they do

Supernatural Entities

CreatureCRDescription
Passion Elemental5Living emotional fire; various emotions
Jealousy Wraith8Ghost of toxic love; attacks happy couples
The Burning One11Person who became pure passion fire
Devotion Construct13Animated by love; serves one purpose forever
Heart Titan17Massive being of solidified passion
Ember Avatar20Kaedris's direct manifestation
---

Travel Times Within the Bastion

JourneyDistanceTime (foot)
Heartsgate → Ember's Rest~180 miles9 days
Heartsgate → Passion Peaks~250 miles12 days
Heartsgate → Forge of Hearts~140 miles7 days
Heartsgate → Jealousy Wastes~300 miles15 days
Border to border (widest)~650 miles32 days
Note: Passion Roads connect major cities — paths that respond to emotional urgency. Strong enough feeling can halve travel time.

---

Related

  • Kaedris the Crimson Matron
  • The Thirteen Sovereigns
  • The Border Marches
  • World Scale Reference
---

"The cold think passion is weakness. They're right — passion makes you vulnerable, reckless, mortal. It also makes you alive. I'll take burning over existing any day." — Heartsgate citizen

> [!info] The Land of Burning Hearts
> The Bastion of Fireblood is where passion becomes geography. Built from the Left Heart of Aedwynn — the Engine of Passion — this territory burns with emotional fire. Love, hate, jealousy, devotion — every feeling has weight here, and strong emotions can literally ignite.

**Organ Base:** The Left Heart (Engine of Passion)
**Law:** Feel deeply. Love completely. Burn for what you believe.

## Geog...

items.title: The Blackfold - Locations
items.content:
The Blackfold — Locations

---

Overview

Info
The Land of Shadows
The Blackfold is where identity becomes uncertain. Built from the Skin and Boundary of Aedwynn — the divine membrane that separated self from other — this territory exists in perpetual twilight. Here, shadows have weight, reflections have opinions, and the line between you and your darker self is dangerously thin.

Organ Base: The Skin and Boundary (Identity, Fear, Reflection) Law: Know yourself, or your shadow will know you instead. Face your truth, or become someone else's lie.

---

Geography Overview

The Blackfold covers approximately 600,000 square miles — roughly the size of Iran. It is characterized by:

  • The Umbral Plains: Lands of permanent twilight
  • The Mirror Lakes: Bodies of water that reflect what isn't there
  • The Shadow Forests: Woods where darkness is solid
  • The Boundary Walls: Cliffs of skin-like membrane
  • The Reflection Depths: Underground caverns of self-confrontation
The climate is eternally overcast — not dark, but never fully light. The sun exists but is always filtered, as if seen through smoked glass. Shadows here are darker than they should be, longer than physics allows, and not always connected to their sources.

---

Major Regions

The Umbral Plains

Type: Twilight grasslands Character: Most populated; permanent dusk

Vast plains where the light never quite reaches full brightness. Communities here have adapted to the eternal twilight — they navigate by shadow patterns, build with dark materials, and accept that their reflections may not always match their movements.

The Mirror Lakes

Type: Reflective water bodies Character: Dangerous; revelatory; avoided

Lakes and ponds that don't reflect what's actually there — they show hidden truths, possible selves, or complete fabrications. Looking into a Mirror Lake might show you who you really are, who you could become, or someone who wants to become you.

The Shadow Forests

Type: Woodland Character: Where shadows become solid

Forests where darkness pools so deeply it becomes tangible. The shadows here have weight, texture, and sometimes intent. Things live in the absolute dark that have never seen light. The trees themselves cast shadows that move independently.

The Boundary Walls

Type: Membrane cliffs Character: Borders; sacred; dangerous

Massive cliffs of material that looks and feels like skin — preserved dermis from the Maker. These walls mark the borders of the Blackfold and contain doorways to... elsewhere. What's on the other side changes depending on who's looking.

---

Key Locations

1. Obscura — The Shadow Capital

Type: Capital City Population: ~310,000 Location: Built within a permanent shadow cast by nothing visible

"Your shadow is your truest self here. Watch it carefully — and watch everyone else's more carefully."

Description

The capital exists in permanent shadow — not darkness, but the soft gray of eternal shade. The city is built from dark stone that absorbs light, with architecture designed to cast interesting shadows. These shadows are the real city; the buildings are just excuses for them to exist.

In Obscura, your shadow has legal status. Crimes can be committed by shadows against shadows. Contracts require both your signature and your shadow's acceptance. The wealthy employ shadow-tamers to ensure their dark reflections remain loyal.

Districts

DistrictCharacter
The Obsidian CoreCenter; Ultharion's palace; deepest shadow
The PenumbraNobility; managed shadows; prestigious
The Gray MarketCommerce; shadow trading; identity services
The Bright SlumsAreas with too much light; poverty
The Mirror QuarterReflection pools; dangerous; monitored
The Separation WardWhere people and shadows are forcibly parted

Notable Locations

LocationDescription
The Obsidian ThroneUltharion's seat; a shadow so dark it hurts
The Truth MirrorsReflections that cannot lie
The Shadow CourtsLegal system for identity crimes
The Bonding HallsWhere shadows are reunited with people
The Black MarketsTrade in stolen shadows and false identities
The LighthousePrison; full illumination; no shadows allowed
---

2. The Mirror Lakes — Reflection Waters

Type: Lake Region / Sacred/Dangerous Sites Population: ~8,000 (wardens, pilgrims, researchers) Character: Show hidden truths; create doppelgangers

"Don't look too long. The reflection might decide it likes existing more than you do."

Description

A region of lakes and pools whose surfaces don't behave like normal water. Instead of reflecting what's there, they reflect:

  • Hidden truths about the viewer
  • Alternative versions of reality
  • People who died near the lake
  • Things that want to exist but can't
  • Nothing at all (the most frightening option)
The Mirror Lakes are pilgrimage sites for those seeking self-knowledge, but they're also incredibly dangerous. Reflections can sometimes step out of the water. When they do, there's suddenly two versions of the same person — and only one can remain real.

Major Lakes

LakeProperty
The Truth PoolShows your true self; often devastating
The Possibility LakeShows who you could become
The Memory WatersShows your past; even forgotten parts
The Void MirrorShows nothing; most feared
The Doppelganger DeepReflections regularly escape here
---

3. The Shadow Forests — Solid Darkness

Type: Forest Region Population: ~45,000 (adapted communities) Character: Darkness has physical form

"The shadows between the trees will grab you if you're not careful. They're not malicious — they're just hungry for shape. Yours will do."

Description

Forests where darkness doesn't just exist — it pools. The spaces between trees fill with shadow so dense you can touch it, shape it, even be trapped by it. The shadows here are raw material, used by skilled craftsmen to make shadow-cloth, shadow-tools, and shadow-servants.

Communities in the forests have learned to work with the darkness. They harvest shadow like lumber, trade in concentrated dark, and keep strictly regulated light sources to prevent their raw materials from dispersing.

Features

FeatureDescription
Shadow GrovesHarvesting areas; darkness farmed
The Absolute DarkDeepest forest; no light survives
Weavers' VillagesCommunities that make shadow-cloth
The Light BreaksClearings; desperately maintained
---

4. The Boundary Walls — Skin Cliffs

Type: Border Formations / Sacred Sites Population: ~25,000 (priests, guards, travelers) Character: Where the self meets the other

"The walls are skin. The Maker's skin. What's on the other side? Depends on who's asking."

Description

Massive cliff formations of preserved dermal tissue — the Maker's skin, stretched and dried into geography. The Boundary Walls mark the edges of the Blackfold but are far more than geography. They're boundaries in the conceptual sense — between self and other, real and imagined, here and elsewhere.

Doors exist in the walls. Where they lead depends on who opens them: their fears, their desires, their identity. Some doors lead to other territories. Some lead to impossible places. Some lead to wherever you least want to go.

Notable Sites

SiteDescription
The First SkinOldest section; holiest
The Door of FearsOpens to your worst nightmare
The Threshold TempleWorship site; identity rites
The Stranger's GateExit used by those leaving forever
The ScarWhere the skin was wounded; unstable
---

5. The Reflection Depths — Underground Caverns

Type: Cave System Population: ~30,000 Character: Self-confrontation; identity work

"The Depths strip away everything you pretend to be. What's left is either your truth or nothing at all."

Description

A vast underground cavern system where every surface is reflective — walls of polished obsidian, pools of mirror-still water, crystals that catch and repeat light. The Depths are used for identity rituals, self-examination, and punishment.

Going deep into the Depths means confronting yourself — every lie you've told, every mask you've worn, every person you've pretended to be. Some emerge integrated and whole. Others emerge as strangers. Many don't emerge at all.

Levels

LevelChallenge
The Outer CavernsMinor reflections; manageable
The Mirror HallsMultiple reflections; disorienting
The Truth ChambersUnavoidable self-knowledge
The Identity MazeGet lost in versions of yourself
The CoreFace your shadow directly
---

6. The Separation Ward — Shadow Prison

Type: Prison / Medical Facility Population: ~15,000 (prisoners, staff) Location: Northern Obscura

"They take your shadow. You don't realize how much of yourself that is until it's gone."

Description

The most feared institution in the Blackfold — a prison where punishment is the removal of your shadow. Losing your shadow means losing part of your identity: your hidden self, your potential for darkness, your depth. Those without shadows become flat, obvious, incapable of mystery.

The Ward also treats shadow-related illness: shadows that have become hostile, shadows that are trying to replace their owners, shadows that have split into multiple entities.

Conditions

ConditionTreatment
Shadow RebellionShadow wants independence
Identity BleedShadow and person merging
Doppelganger EmergenceReflection stepped out
Shadow LossCriminal punishment; irreversible
---

7. The Black Markets — Shadow Commerce

Type: Commercial District Population: ~35,000 Location: Gray Market District, Obscura

"I can sell you a shadow if you've lost yours. I can buy yours if you're willing. I can get you someone else's if you're discrete."

Description

The commercial heart of shadow trade — buying, selling, and renting shadows. Legal shadow commerce includes shadow-cloth, shadow-servants, and identity confirmation services. Illegal commerce includes stolen shadows, false identities, and doppelganger trafficking.

The Black Markets are where criminals come to become someone else, where the shadowless come hoping for restoration, and where those with too much shadow come to shed the excess.

---

8. The Lighthouse — Maximum Illumination Prison

Type: Prison Population: ~3,000 (prisoners, guards) Location: Artificial island of total light

"No shadows. No darkness. No hiding. For those who've lived their lives in shade, it's the worst torture imaginable."

Description

A prison built on an artificial island flooded with magical light from every angle. There are no shadows here — not from buildings, not from bodies, not from anything. For Blackfold natives, this is torture. For shadow-criminals, this is appropriate punishment.

Prisoners in the Lighthouse go slowly mad from exposure. With no shadow, they feel exposed — every thought visible, every flaw illuminated, every secret revealed. Most confess to crimes they didn't commit just to feel hidden again.

---

Society & Culture

The Shadow Hierarchy

ClassRoleNotes
The Obsidian ShepherdSovereignUltharion; knows all shadows
The Deep ShadedInner courtShadows so dark they're independent
Shadow LordsNobilityFamilies with prestigious shadows
The PenumbralMiddle classWell-managed shadows
CitizensGeneral populationNormal shadow relationships
The FlatLower classWeak or stolen shadows
The ExposedPrisoners/outcastsShadowless

Shadow Rights

In the Blackfold, shadows have legal status:

  • Shadows can own property
  • Shadows can commit crimes (punished through the owner)
  • Shadows can testify in court
  • Shadows can inherit
  • Contracts require shadow consent
  • Shadow murder is punishable by death

Identity Verification

Your identity is confirmed by your shadow:

  • Travel documents include shadow prints
  • Banking requires shadow verification
  • Marriage binds shadows as well as people
  • Death is confirmed when the shadow fades
---

Creatures of the Blackfold

Native Fauna

CreatureDescription
Shadow CatFeline that exists only as shadow; loyal
Mirror FishSwims in reflection lakes; edible; causes visions
Darkness MothConsumes light; used for shadow farming
The UnlightedAnimals born without shadows; pitied
Penumbral StalkerPredator that hunts from shadow

Supernatural Entities

CreatureCRDescription
Living Shadow5Shadow without an owner; seeks one
Reflection Wraith8Escaped mirror duplicate
Identity Thief11Steals shadows; wears multiple
The Doppelganger13Perfect duplicate; wants to replace
Shadow Elemental15Living darkness; obscures everything
The Obsidian One19Ultharion's direct servant
---

Travel Times Within the Blackfold

JourneyDistanceTime (foot)
Obscura → Mirror Lakes~280 miles14 days
Obscura → Shadow Forests~200 miles10 days
Obscura → Boundary Walls~350 miles17 days
Border to border~780 miles39 days
Note: Travel at night is easier (shadows are friendlier in darkness). Bright days are difficult.

---

Related

  • Ultharion the Obsidian Shepherd
  • The Thirteen Sovereigns
  • The Border Marches
  • World Scale Reference
---

"You think your shadow is just darkness in your shape. Here, you learn it's the other way around — you're light in your shadow's shape. The shadow was there first." — Obscura philosopher

items.title: The Blooming Rot - Locations
items.content:
The Blooming Rot — Locations

---

Overview

Info
The Living Forest
The Blooming Rot is not a forest — it is Talaris Bloomrend's domain, grown from the Liver of Aedwynn the Maker. Every location serves a purpose in the cycle of decay and rebirth. Pilgrims walk established paths. Those who stray rarely return unchanged.

Organ Base: The Liver (Regeneration Core) Law: All must decay. From decay comes rebirth. The cycle cannot be halted.

---

Map Reference — The Pilgrim's Path

The established route from the border to Verdant Pass:

``` BORDER ↓ [1] SPOREHAVEN (waystation) ↓ [2] THE SWEETWATER MARSH (crossing) ↓ [3] THE PILGRIM'S SPINE (ridge path) ↓ [4] THE HUNGER GARDEN (dangerous) ↓ [5] THE CONGREGATION GROVE (sacred) ↓ [6] VERDANT PASS — "THE WOUND" (destination) ```

Off the Path:

  • [7] The Mulching Grounds (west)
  • [8] The Bower of Whispers (east)
  • [9] The Nursery (deep forest)
  • [10] The Throne of Roots (center — Talaris's seat)
  • [11] The Hollow (anomaly)
  • [12] The Boneyard Glade (mass grave)
---

ON THE PILGRIM'S PATH

---

1. Sporehaven

Type: Pilgrim Waystation CR Range: 0-2 (safe zone) Distance from Border: Half-day travel

"Rest here. The King tolerates this place. Beyond, you belong to the forest."

Description

A village of fungal huts and moss-draped shelters clustered around a massive dead tree — the Pilgrim's Rest. The tree's hollow trunk serves as a common room, its interior lit by bioluminescent fungi. The air smells of damp earth and cooking spores.

Sporehaven exists because Talaris allows it. Pilgrims need a staging point before entering his domain proper. The villagers are a mix of permanent residents (those who came seeking the King and settled for proximity) and transient pilgrims preparing for the journey deeper.

Notable Features

FeatureDescription
The Pilgrim's RestHollowed tree serving as inn — 4 gold/night, moss beds, hookah room
The Elder's HutWhere the old woman lives who warned the party about honesty
The Offering PoolPilgrims leave gifts for Talaris here — food, blood, memories
The Border StonesMarks the edge of the "safe" zone — beyond, the forest hunts

Encounters

  • Pilgrims seeking the King's blessing
  • Merchants selling fungal goods, antitoxins, and "safe passage charms" (mostly fake)
  • The occasional Choir Bloom observing from the treeline
  • Bloom Hounds that have learned to beg for scraps

Current Party Status

The party is HERE as of Session 18+. They've rented a room, fed the Bleeding Blade a rabbit, and learned the Verdant King is already tracking them.

---

2. The Sweetwater Marsh

Type: Crossing / Transition Zone CR Range: 2-5 Distance from Sporehaven: 3-4 hours travel

"The water looks clean. Smells clean. Tastes sweet, they say. Don't drink it."

Description

A low-lying wetland where the forest floor gives way to black water and tussock islands. The water is crystal clear — deceptively so. It's filtered through miles of fungal root systems that remove impurities but add something else. Something that makes you want to stay.

The Sweetwater is a transition zone. Pilgrims cross via a path of raised roots and fallen logs maintained by the Choir Blooms. Those who stray from the path, or drink the water, often don't leave.

Notable Features

FeatureDescription
The Root PathA winding trail of interlocked roots — the only safe crossing
The Sweet SpringsBubbling pools of crystal water — DC 14 WIS save or compelled to drink
The Dreaming StonesPilgrims who drank and never left — calcified, overgrown, still breathing
The Watching LiliesCorpse Lily Lurkers disguised among normal water flowers

Hazards

Sweetwater Compulsion: Creatures that drink must succeed on a DC 14 Wisdom save or become charmed by the marsh. They want to stay. They find reasons to linger. After 24 hours, the save increases to DC 16. After 48 hours, they begin to calcify.

Unstable Footing: Leaving the Root Path requires DC 13 Acrobatics checks every 60 feet or fall into the water (10 feet deep, difficult terrain to swim).

Encounters

d6Encounter
1-21d4 Corpse Lily Lurkers (ambush from water)
3-42d6 Rot Hares (drinking safely — they're adapted)
51 Gullet Vine (underwater, tendrils rising)
6A Dreaming Stone that speaks — a pilgrim who remembers the way
---

3. The Pilgrim's Spine

Type: Ridge Path / Transition Zone CR Range: 3-6 Distance from Sweetwater Marsh: 4-5 hours travel

"The Spine is safe. The Spine is watched. Stay on the Spine."

Description

A long, winding ridge of compacted earth and ancient root systems that rises above the forest floor. The Pilgrim's Spine is the safest route through the mid-forest — elevated above the worst predators, maintained by the Rooted Ones, and clearly marked with fungal lanterns that glow at night.

The path is narrow — single file in places. To either side, the forest drops away into shadow. Things move down there. Things that look up at the lit path and wait for someone to fall.

Notable Features

FeatureDescription
The Lantern PostsBioluminescent fungi on stakes — mark the safe path
The Watchers' PerchesPlatforms where Choir Blooms observe pilgrims
The Fallen ShrineA collapsed section where a Rooted One died — now a memorial
The OverlookHighest point — view of the Hunger Garden below

Hazards

Narrow Path: Some sections require DC 12 Acrobatics to navigate without risk. Failure means sliding toward the edge — DC 14 DEX save to catch yourself.

The Drop: Falling off the Spine is a 30-60 foot drop into dense undergrowth — 3d6 falling damage, and you're now off the path with no easy way back up.

Encounters

d6Encounter
1-21d4 Choir Blooms (checking pilgrim intentions)
3-41 Rot Tiger (stalking below, watching)
52d4 Bloom Hounds (running alongside, curious)
6A pilgrim who fell and climbed back — wounded, terrified, changed
---

4. The Hunger Garden

Type: Predator Territory / Dangerous Crossing Distance from Pilgrim's Spine: 6-8 hours travel (if lucky)

"Everything in the Garden is hungry. Including the ground."

Description

A vast basin where the forest is wrong. The canopy is so thick that no sunlight reaches the floor. Everything is lit by bioluminescence — beautiful and deadly. The plants here have learned that animals are food. The animals have learned that plants are food. Everything eats everything.

The Hunger Garden is Talaris's digestive system at its most active. Organic matter enters. Nutrients exit. The process is rarely pleasant for the organic matter.

Notable Features

FeatureDescription
The DescentWhere the Spine path drops into the Garden — no going back easily
The Bone TrailPrevious pilgrims' remains mark the "safe" path through
The Maw FieldsArea dense with Maw of the Garden pit traps
The Feeding GroundsWhere Thornback Behemoths come to eat — everything avoids them
The Exit RiseThe climb out toward the Congregation Grove

Hazards

No Light: Natural darkness. Only bioluminescence and the party's light sources. Creatures here have Blindsight or Darkvision.

Active Predation: Random encounter checks every 30 minutes instead of every hour.

The Ground Itself: Maw of the Garden traps are DC 18 Perception to notice. Gullet Vines are DC 16. Corpse Lily Lurkers are DC 15.

Encounters

d8Encounter
11 Gullet Vine (blocking path)
21 Rot Tiger (ambush)
31d4+1 Planted (wandering, speaking fragments)
41 Maw of the Garden (pit trap)
51 Thornback Behemoth (feeding — avoid or fight)
62d4 Compost Crawlers + 1 Gullet Vine
71 Rot Tiger + pack of 1d6 Bloom Hounds
8A dying pilgrim being slowly digested by a Corpse Lily Lurker
---

5. The Congregation Grove

Type: Sacred Site / Social Encounter Zone Distance from Hunger Garden: 2-3 hours travel

"The Grove is where Talaris listens. Speak truth, or speak nothing."

Description

A ring of thirteen massive trees — each one over 200 feet tall — surrounding a clearing of soft moss and perpetual green twilight. The air here is thick with spores that think. The Choir Blooms worship here. The Rooted Ones remember here. The King hears everything spoken in this place.

The Congregation Grove is a checkpoint. Pilgrims who reach it have proven they can survive the Garden. Now they must prove they deserve to reach the Wound.

Notable Features

FeatureDescription
The Thirteen TreesAncient giants — each one a Rooted One's final form
The Moss CircleCentral gathering space — speaking here reaches Talaris
The Altar of GrowthWhere offerings are made — blood, flesh, memories
The Warden GatesTwo Grove Wardens flank the exit toward Verdant Pass

The Questioning

When the party enters, Choir Blooms will emerge and ask questions:

1. "What do you carry into the King's domain?" 2. "What do you seek at the Wound?" 3. "Who sent you, and why?" 4. "What would you offer for passage?"

Honest answers — even uncomfortable ones — are rewarded with safe passage. Lies — even small ones — are detected (the spores read thoughts). The consequence scales with the lie.

Encounters

d6Encounter
1-22d4 Choir Blooms (the questioning)
3-41 Rooted One (offers information for a price)
51 Grove Warden (blocking the exit — demands proof of honesty)
6A Rooted One who knew Elias — or knew OF him
---

6. Verdant Pass — "The Wound"

Type: Anomaly / Destination CR Range: Variable (the Wound itself is CR 0 — what guards it varies) Distance from Congregation Grove: Half-day travel

"The Wound is where something cut the King. He healed around it, but never closed it."

Description

A scar in the forest. A place where the Blooming Rot simply stops.

Verdant Pass is a narrow ravine cutting through the heart of Talaris's domain — and the forest refuses to grow there. The walls are bare stone, grey and dead. No moss. No fungi. No life. Just rock and silence and a path that leads somewhere the King cannot follow.

The Wound was made when Talaris claimed this territory. Something resisted. Something cut him. The cut healed around the resistance but never consumed it.

Elias Alethain's body lies somewhere in the Wound. He went there to die where Talaris couldn't claim him.

Notable Features

FeatureDescription
The ThresholdWhere forest meets stone — a visible line of demarcation
The Silent PathThe ravine floor — unnaturally quiet, no birdsong, no insects
The Cave of WaitingWhere Elias went to die — marked by nothing but stone
The ExitThe other side — into lands beyond Talaris's domain

What the Party Finds

When they reach the Cave of Waiting:

  • Elias Alethain's skeleton, untouched by decay
  • No fungal growth, no rot, no reclamation
  • Mira's wedding ring (if Sol places it) will rest beside him
  • A sense of profound silence — the first true quiet since entering the Blooming Rot
---

OFF THE PILGRIM'S PATH

---

7. The Mulching Grounds

Type: Corpse Processing Zone Location: West of the Pilgrim's Spine

"Where the dead become the living. Don't go there unless you want to join the process."

Description

A vast depression where the forest processes its dead. Everything that dies in the Blooming Rot eventually ends up here — dragged by Compost Crawlers, carried by Planted, or simply rolling downhill into the basin.

The smell is indescribable.

Notable Features

FeatureDescription
The Intake SlopesWhere corpses roll/are dragged in
The Processing LayersStrata of decomposition — recent on top, soil at bottom
The Crawler WarrensTunnel networks where Compost Crawlers breed
The Bone ReefA ridge of accumulated bones the crawlers can't process

Hazards

The Stench: DC 14 Constitution save every hour or gain 1 level of exhaustion from nausea.

Unstable Ground: The "floor" is decomposing matter. DC 14 DEX save or sink up to waist.

Disease: Exposure risk for Rotspore is constant. Save every hour.

Encounters

d6Encounter
1-23d6 Compost Crawlers (disturbed)
3-42d4 Planted (shambling through, lost)
51 Thornback Behemoth (feeding)
6A living pilgrim, half-buried, begging for help
---

8. The Bower of Whispers

Type: Memory Repository / Psychic Hazard Zone Location: East of the Congregation Grove

"The forest remembers everything. The Bower is where it keeps the memories it can't digest."

Description

A grove of pale, leafless trees with bark that looks like stretched skin. The air is thick with spores that carry memories — not thoughts, but experiences. Walking through the Bower means experiencing fragments of everyone who ever died here.

Notable Features

FeatureDescription
The Skin TreesPale bark with vaguely facial patterns
The Memory PoolsDepressions filled with luminescent spores
The Choir's ArchiveWhere Choir Blooms come to access old memories
The Screaming HollowA concentration of traumatic deaths — avoid

Effects

Passive Memory Bleed: While in the Bower, characters experience random memory fragments. Wisdom save DC 13 each hour or gain a random memory that isn't yours.

Memory Pools: Deliberately inhaling pool spores allows access to specific memories — but requires DC 16 Wisdom save or become lost in them (incapacitated for 1d4 hours).

Encounters

d6Encounter
1-22d4 Choir Blooms (archivists, may help or hinder)
3-41d4 Planted (drawn to memories of their lives)
51 Bloom Mother (she collects specific memories)
6A Memory Storm — DC 16 WIS save or relive a death
---

9. The Nursery

Type: Bloom Mother Territory / Spawning Ground Location: Deep Forest, south of the Hunger Garden

"Where the new things grow. Where the old things go to become mothers."

Description

A vast hollow where the Bloom Mothers birth their children. The ground is soft with spore-matter. The air is so thick with reproductive material that breathing without protection means certain infection.

Notable Features

FeatureDescription
The Mother Towers3-5 Bloom Mother structures in various stages of growth
The Birthing PoolsWhere new creatures emerge, glistening and hungry
The Feeding TroughsCorpses brought here to nourish the young
The Elder MotherThe oldest Bloom Mother — possibly pre-Usurpation

Hazards

Constant Infection Risk: DC 16 Constitution save every 10 minutes or contract Bloom Sickness.

Protective Swarms: 4d6 Sporelings attack anything that threatens a Mother.

Encounters

d6Encounter
1-24d6 Sporelings + 2d4 Planted (nursery defenders)
3-41 Bloom Mother (parley possible)
52 Bloom Mothers (territorial dispute — party caught in middle)
6The Elder Mother speaks — she wants something
---

10. The Throne of Roots

Type: Talaris's Seat of Power Location: Center of the Blooming Rot

"You don't go to the Throne. The Throne comes to you, if the King wills it."

Description

The heart of Talaris Bloomrend's domain. A mountain-sized mass of interwoven roots, fungal towers, and living wood. At its center, the Verdant King is — not sitting, but being. The Throne is where his consciousness is most concentrated.

Notable Features

FeatureDescription
The ApproachMiles of increasingly dense forest — the King watching every step
The Root GatesMassive archways — Grove Wardens guard each one
The Audience ChamberA hollow where supplicants are received
The HeartThe actual remnant of Aedwynn's Liver — pulsing, alive, divine

What Happens Here

If the party reaches the Throne (or is brought here), they face Talaris himself. Not a Herald. Talaris.

---

11. The Hollow

Type: Anomaly / Dead Zone CR Range: Variable Location: Northeast, near the border

"There's a place where the forest doesn't grow. Where Talaris doesn't reach. We don't go there."

Description

A perfect circle of dead earth. Nothing grows. Nothing decays. Things that enter simply... stop.

The Hollow is a wound in reality — a place where the Liver's regenerative power doesn't work.

Notable Features

FeatureDescription
The Dead RingThe border — forest on one side, nothing on the other
The PreservedThings that died inside — perfectly preserved, never rotting
The SilenceNo sound carries inside the Hollow
The CrackA fissure at the center — no one knows how deep

Effects

No Healing: Healing magic doesn't work. Potions have no effect. Natural recovery stops.

No Decay: Nothing rots. Corpses are perfectly preserved.

No Sound: Complete silence. Speech requires lip-reading or telepathy.

The Crack: Looking into it requires DC 18 Wisdom save or be compelled to walk toward it.

---

12. The Boneyard Glade

Type: Mass Grave / Historical Site Location: West of Verdant Pass

"An army came here once. Tried to burn the forest. The forest ate them all."

Description

A vast clearing filled with bones. Thousands of them. The remains of an army that tried to purge the Blooming Rot three centuries ago — all consumed and left as a monument.

The bones whisper at night. The Planted that rose here still remember being soldiers.

Notable Features

FeatureDescription
The Bone FieldAcres of skeletal remains, rusted weapons, rotted banners
The General's MoundWhere the commander fell — his Planted form still gives orders
The Siege LineAbandoned catapults and ballistae, overgrown
The Burning GroundWhere they used fire — the forest grew back wrong here

The Soldier-Planted

Unlike normal Planted, these retain military discipline. They patrol in formation. They challenge intruders. They try to complete their mission: burn the forest.

They can't. They're part of it now. But they don't know that.

Encounters

d6Encounter
1-22d6 Soldier-Planted (patrol, will challenge)
3-41d4 Soldier-Planted + 1 Officer-Planted (giving orders)
6A Thornback Behemoth (picking through bones)
---

TRAVEL REFERENCE

Pilgrim's Path Travel Times

FromToTimeDifficulty
BorderSporehavenHalf-dayEasy
SporehavenSweetwater Marsh3-4 hoursEasy
Sweetwater MarshPilgrim's Spine4-5 hoursModerate
Pilgrim's SpineHunger Garden6-8 hoursDangerous
Hunger GardenCongregation Grove2-3 hoursModerate
Congregation GroveVerdant PassHalf-dayEasy (if permitted)
Total Border to Verdant Pass: Approximately 3-4 days with rest stops.

---

Random Event Table (While Traveling)

d20Event
1-5Nothing — the forest watches
6-8Weather change — bioluminescent rain (harmless, beautiful)
9-10Pilgrim group — 1d6 travelers heading the same/opposite way
11-12Shrine — small offering site with gifts left by previous pilgrims
13-14Fresh kill — something died here recently, scavengers arriving
15-16The path splits — one way looks faster but less traveled
17-18Choir Bloom observer — watching from distance, doesn't approach
19The ground shifts — Talaris redirecting the path subtly
20The King speaks — voice emerges from the forest, asks one question
---

Related

  • Blooming Rot Bestiary
  • Talaris Bloomrend the Verdant King
  • Sporehaven
  • Verdant Pass
  • Mira Alethain
  • Elias Alethain
  • Campaign Sessions
---

Every step in the Blooming Rot is borrowed. The forest lets you walk because it chooses to. When it stops choosing, you stop walking.
items.raw:
# The Blooming Rot — Locations

---

## Overview

> [!info] The Living Forest
> The Blooming Rot is not a forest — it is **Talaris Bloomrend's domain**, grown from the Liver of Aedwynn the Maker. Every location serves a purpose in the cycle of decay and rebirth. Pilgrims walk established paths. Those who stray rarely return unchanged.

**Organ Base:** The Liver (Regeneration Core)
**Law:** All must decay. From decay comes rebirth. The cycle cannot be halted.

---

## Map Reference — The Pilgrim'...

items.title: The Clockwork Sanctum - Locations
items.content:
The Clockwork Sanctum — Locations

---

Overview

Info
The Land of Measured Time
The Clockwork Sanctum is where time became mechanical. Built from the Heart of Time — Aedwynn's temporal organ — this territory exists in precisely regulated moments. Every second is accounted for. Every minute has purpose. And every debt to time must eventually be paid.

Organ Base: The Heart of Time (Temporal Regulation) Law: Time is justice. All debts are paid. All crimes are punished. Eventually.

---

Geography Overview

The Clockwork Sanctum covers approximately 280,000 square miles — the smallest stable Sovereign territory, roughly the size of Turkey. It is characterized by:

  • The Clockwork Plains: Flat expanses where time flows predictably
  • The Gear Mountains: Massive mechanical formations from the transformed Heart
  • The Frozen Moments: Regions where time has stopped entirely
  • The Loop Zones: Areas trapped in temporal repetition
  • The Pendulum Valleys: Where time swings between fast and slow
The climate is temperate but regulated — seasons change on exact schedules, weather follows predetermined patterns, and the sun rises at the same precise second every day.

---

Major Regions

The Clockwork Plains

Type: Central territory Character: Predictable time; most populated

The safest region, where Imhuran's control is absolute and time flows at exactly one second per second. Cities and farms thrive here because tomorrow reliably follows today. The price: everything is scheduled, regulated, and accounted for.

The Gear Mountains

Type: Mountain range Character: The Heart's mechanical transformation

Where the Heart of Time transformed into clockwork, massive gear-shaped mountains dominate the landscape. They turn slowly — a rotation taking decades — grinding time itself into predictable intervals. The grinding sound is constant, like the ticking of an impossible clock.

The Frozen Moments

Type: Temporal anomalies Character: Time stopped; dangerous; preserved

Regions where time has simply stopped. People, animals, and objects frozen mid-action. You can walk through these areas (your time continues), but touching the frozen risks pulling you into their stasis. The Frozen Moments are museums of history — and prisons for Imhuran's enemies.

The Loop Zones

Type: Temporal traps Character: Repeating time; prisons

Areas where time repeats — the same hour, day, or year cycling endlessly. Some are natural anomalies. Others were created as punishment. The inhabitants don't always know they're looping. From inside, each cycle feels like the first time.

---

Key Locations

1. Chronopolis — The Ticking Capital

Type: Capital City Population: ~320,000 Location: Built within and around the Great Gear

"Every citizen knows their schedule. Every schedule serves the Sanctum. Every service pays your debt to time."

Description

Chronopolis is built around the Great Gear — a mountain-sized clockwork mechanism that regulates time throughout the Sanctum. The city rises in concentric rings, each ring synchronized to a different temporal schedule. The innermost rings run slightly faster (more productive time for the elite); the outermost slightly slower (laborers have longer days).

Life in Chronopolis is rigidly scheduled. Wake times, meal times, work times, and sleep times are assigned at birth and adjusted based on your value to the Sanctum. Being late is a crime. Wasting time is theft — theft from Imhuran himself.

Districts

DistrictCharacter
The Gear CoreCenter; Imhuran's court; fastest time
The Hour HandNobility; government; slightly fast
The Minute WardCommerce; professional class; standard time
The Second RingWorking class; slightly slow
The Pendulum SlumsPoorest; unstable time; dangerous
The Frozen ArchiveCriminals in temporal stasis

Notable Locations

LocationDescription
The Throne of HoursImhuran's seat; controls time throughout the Sanctum
The Debt RegistryRecords all temporal debts owed by citizens
The Temporal CourtsJustice system; crimes judged across past and future
The Great GearThe transformed Heart; the city's foundation
The Schedule HallWhere citizens receive their life itineraries
The Adjustment ChambersWhere schedules are "corrected"
---

2. The Frozen Moments — Temporal Prisons

Type: Anomaly Zones / Prison System Population: Unknown (frozen inhabitants don't count) Character: Time stopped; living museum; eternal punishment

"Fursen spent six hundred years in one of these. A blink to us. An eternity to him."

Description

Scattered throughout the Sanctum are regions where time has stopped — not slowed, stopped. These are both natural phenomena and deliberate prisons. Imhuran "freezes" criminals, dissidents, and inconvenient people, leaving them in eternal stasis until he decides to release them.

The Frozen Moments are also museums. Historical events are preserved mid-occurrence. Visitors can walk through ancient battles, observe extinct creatures, or study moments that changed history — all frozen in perfect clarity.

Major Frozen Sites

SiteContentsAge
The Battle EternalArmy frozen mid-charge~800 years
The Feast of ThornsAssassination attempt captured~400 years
The First MomentOldest freeze; unknown contentsPre-Usurpation?
The Traitor's GalleryPolitical prisonersVarious
Fursen's PrisonWhere Fursen was held~600 years
---

3. The Loop Zones — Repeating Hells

Type: Temporal Anomalies / Prisons Character: Time cycles endlessly; inmates don't always know

"The worst part isn't living the same day forever. The worst part is hoping this time will be different. It never is."

Description

Areas where time loops — repeating the same period indefinitely. Natural Loop Zones are usually short (minutes to hours) and avoidable. Artificial ones, created by Imhuran as punishment, can be years long.

The inhabitants of Loop Zones often don't realize they're trapped. From inside, each cycle feels fresh. Only certain individuals — "Loop Aware" — retain memories across iterations. These are either the intentional prisoners (who know their punishment) or random victims of the loop (who slowly go insane).

Known Loops

LoopDurationPurpose
The Minute Loop60 secondsNatural; tourist attraction
The Day Prison24 hoursCommon punishment loop
The Year of Regret1 yearSevere punishment
The Wedding Loop6 hoursTragedy; natural; being studied
The Lost Century100 yearsContains an entire civilization
---

4. The Gear Mountains — The Heart's Form

Type: Mountain Range / Sacred Site Population: ~45,000 (monks, engineers, pilgrims) Character: Where time is manufactured

"The gears turn whether we watch or not. Our job is to ensure they turn correctly."

Description

The Heart of Time didn't become landscape — it became machinery. The Gear Mountains are massive interlocking cogs, springs, and escapements, each the size of a conventional mountain. They turn slowly, grinding against each other, producing the regulated time that flows through the Sanctum.

Temporal Engineers live here, maintaining the cosmic clockwork. Monks worship the Great Mechanism. Pilgrims come to synchronize their personal time with the universal standard.

Sites

SitePurpose
The First GearLargest; considered holiest
The Spring ValleysMassive mainsprings; energy storage
The Escapement TempleWhere time's "ticks" originate
The Engineer MonasteryTraining facility for temporal maintenance
The Calibration PeaksWhere time corrections are calculated
---

5. The Debt Registry — Administrative Center

Type: Government Building Population: ~12,000 (administrators, clerks) Location: Central Chronopolis

"Everyone owes time. From your first breath, you're in debt. Life is paying it back."

Description

The central administrative hub of the Sanctum, where every citizen's temporal debt is tracked. In Imhuran's philosophy, existence itself is borrowed time — you owe the universe for every moment of consciousness. The Debt Registry tracks these debts and ensures payment.

Payment comes in forms of labor, service, sacrifice, or — for serious debtors — time itself (years removed from your lifespan). The truly indebted are frozen until their "interest" is calculated and collected.

Functions

FunctionDescription
Birth RegistrationInitial debt assessment
Life SchedulingAssigned schedules for optimal debt payment
Debt CollectionReclaiming owed time
Death ProcessingFinal accounting; inheritance of debt
AppealsRarely successful
---

6. The Temporal Courts — Justice System

Type: Court Complex Population: ~8,000 (judges, advocates, prisoners) Character: Crimes judged across all time

"You didn't commit the crime yet. But you will. We've seen it. Justice doesn't wait for guilt to become convenient."

Description

Imhuran's courts don't just judge past crimes — they judge future ones. Temporal seers examine timelines, identifying crimes before they occur. Pre-criminals are detained, tried, and punished for acts they haven't yet committed but would have without intervention.

This is not popular, but it is effective. Crime rates in the Sanctum are the lowest in the Ashen Realms.

Court Types

CourtJurisdiction
The Past CourtConventional crimes already committed
The Present CourtCrimes in progress
The Future CourtCrimes that will/would occur
The Loop CourtCrimes within temporal anomalies
The Paradox TribunalCrimes against time itself
---

7. The Pendulum Valleys — Unstable Time

Type: Dangerous Region Population: ~60,000 (adapted communities) Character: Time swings between fast and slow

"Monday lasted three weeks. Tuesday was over in an hour. You learn to pack appropriately."

Description

Regions where time doesn't flow steadily but swings between extremes — days that last seconds, seconds that last days. The Pendulum Valleys are considered undesirable, home to those who can't afford regulated time or those who prefer unpredictability.

Communities here have adapted. They watch the "time weather" and plan accordingly. Long-time periods are for construction and farming; short-time periods are for rest and waiting. The unpredictable rhythm creates a culture that's surprisingly relaxed about deadlines.

---

8. The Adjustment Chambers — Correction Facilities

Type: Prison / Medical Facility Population: ~20,000 (inmates, staff) Location: Beneath Chronopolis

"You were late seventeen times. Your schedule clearly doesn't suit you. We'll find one that does."

Description

Not everyone adapts to the Sanctum's rigid scheduling. The Adjustment Chambers are where citizens whose behavior doesn't match their assigned schedule are "corrected." This involves temporal therapy (experiencing time at different rates until you synchronize), schedule reassignment (usually to worse positions), or in extreme cases, freezing.

The Chambers are not cruel by intent — Imhuran genuinely believes he's helping people find their proper place in time. The results, however, often involve significant psychological damage.

---

Society & Culture

The Temporal Hierarchy

ClassRoleTime Rate
The Regent of HoursSovereignControls all
The CalibratedInner courtFastest (most time)
Temporal EngineersPriests/techniciansFast
The ScheduledCitizensStandard
The IndebtedHeavy debtorsSlow
The FrozenPrisonersStopped
The LostLoop inhabitantsRepeating

Time as Currency

In the Sanctum, time is literally money:

  • Citizens earn "hours" through labor
  • Hours can be spent on goods, services, or added to lifespan
  • Debt is measured in time owed
  • The wealthy live longer (they can afford more time)
  • The poor die young (they've spent their allocation)

The Schedule

Every citizen receives a life schedule at birth:

  • Wake time, work time, meal time, sleep time
  • Major life events (marriage, children, death) pre-scheduled
  • Deviations are crimes
  • Schedules can be adjusted through petition (rarely granted)
---

Creatures of the Sanctum

Native Fauna

CreatureDescription
Clockwork BeetleMechanical insects; keep time naturally
Time MothWings show different moments; beautiful
Pendulum BirdSwings through time zones; hard to catch
Gear SnakeLives in the machinery; partially mechanical
The AgelessCreatures outside time; neither young nor old

Supernatural Entities

CreatureCRDescription
Time Shade5Ghost from another timeline
Loop Wraith8Spirit trapped in repetition
Clockwork Guardian11Construct maintaining temporal order
The Desynchronized13People unstuck from time
Temporal Elemental15Living time; speeds/slows at will
Yesterday's Echo17Entity from erased timeline
The Regent's Hand20Imhuran's direct enforcer
---

Travel Times Within the Sanctum

JourneyDistanceTime (foot)Notes
Chronopolis → Gear Mountains~180 miles9 daysStandard time
Chronopolis → Pendulum Valleys~220 milesVariableDepends on swing
Border to border~530 miles26 daysAssuming no anomalies
Note: Time anomalies can drastically alter travel duration. A journey through a Frozen Moment takes no time at all (you walk through). A journey through a Loop Zone might never end.

---

Related

  • Imhuran the Regent of Hours
  • The Thirteen Sovereigns
  • The Border Marches
  • World Scale Reference
  • Fursen (was imprisoned here)
---

"Other lands fear death. We don't — death is just the final entry in your schedule. We fear being late for it." — Chronopolis citizen
