---

## Overview

> [!info] Beyond Divine Rule
> Not all of the Ashen Realms bows to the Thirteen. Approximately **8 million people** live in cities, towns, and settlements that exist outside direct Sovereign control — in Border Marches, neutral zones, or territories so remote or worthless that no Sovereign bothered to claim them.

The Free Cities are not utopias. They're often more dangerous than Sovereign territories — lawless, resource-poor, and constantly t...

items.title: History & Timeline
items.content:
History & Timeline

The history of the Ashen Realms is measured not in years, but in fractures—moments when reality broke and reformed around new truths. Since the murder of Aedwynn the Maker, time itself has become unreliable, looping in some regions, frozen in others, racing forward in still more.

---

The Nine Eras

Era I: The Era of Aedwynn's Breath (Creation)

Duration: Unknown (predates measurable time) Status: Mythical

The beginning. Aedwynn the Maker shaped the world from living organs, breathing existence into cosmic flesh. The world was perfect, terrible, and singular—a god's body made manifest as landscape.

Key Events:

  • The Maker's first breath
  • Geography as anatomy
  • Perfect unity of matter and divinity
  • No death, no time, no change
End Condition: The Thirteen Conspirators discovered the location of the Maker's physical form.

---

Era II: The Era of the Thirteenfold Murder (The Usurpation)

Duration: A single moment stretched across centuries Status: Historical fact, mythologized beyond recognition

Thirteen mortals—historians, priests, philosophers, warriors, surgeons—discovered they could kill their god. They did not do this impulsively. It was ritual. Calculated. Obsessive.

The Thirteen Killers: 1. Azerach the Whispered King - Severed the Lungs 2. Imhuran the Regent of Hours - Carved out the Heart of Time 3. Mareatha the Vein-Mother - Ripped free the Circulatory Engine 4. Eredain the Choirless Monarch - Stole the Resonance Organ 5. Vortegas the Bone-Warden - Claimed the Skeletal Foundation 6. Kaedris the Crimson Matron - Seized the Engine of Passion 7. Aral-Vyn the Bladed Saint - Harvested the Musculature 8. Vor'Kael the Hollow Empress - Consumed the Digestive System 9. Ultharion the Obsidian Shepherd - Peeled the Skin and Membranes 10. Ismara the Pale Duchess - Extracted the Brain and Neural System 11. Talaris Bloomrend the Verdant King - Took the Decay Organs 12. Nhalyra the Azure Queen - Submerged into the Soulflow 13. Karthayne the Hollow Crown - Grasped the Heart of Creation

Consequences:

  • Reality fractured
  • The Maker's corpse became the landscape
  • Each killer transformed into a Sovereign
  • The organs became realms
  • Time, space, and meaning destabilized
End Condition: The last organ was claimed. Aedwynn died. The Thirteen ascended.

---

Era III: The Era of Thirteen Kingdoms

Duration: ~2,000 years Status: The Age of Division

Each Sovereign retreated to their claimed organ and began reshaping it into a domain reflecting their obsession. Nations formed. Laws were written. Reality bent to match each ruler's vision.

Key Events:

  • Veinspire Cathedral constructed
  • The Clockwork Sanctum manifests
  • The Dominion of Still Air silences entire kingdoms
  • Ossathar's bone cities rise
  • Soulflow begins leaking into the ocean
Cultural Impact:
  • Mortals fractured into regional identities
  • Species began diverging
  • Magic became organ-specific
  • Worship replaced understanding
End Condition: The Sovereigns began noticing each other's existence and influence bleeding across borders.

---

Era IV: The Era of Shattered Reality

Duration: ~500 years (subjective; actual duration unknown) Status: The Great Collapse

The organs were not meant to be separated. Their division caused cascading failures in the fabric of existence.

Manifestations:

  • Gravity collapsed in Eredain's territories
  • Time looped in Imhuran's sanctum
  • Dreams flooded waking reality near Nhalyra's ocean
  • Digestive storms dissolved meaning in Vor'Kael's expanse
  • Entire populations lost language near Azerach's domain
Mortal Response:
  • Mass migration
  • City-states collapse
  • Survival-focused cultures emerge
  • "Reality anchors" discovered (objects stabilizing local physics)
End Condition: The Sovereigns grudgingly established territorial boundaries to prevent total annihilation.

---

Era V: The Era of Veinspire's Dominion

Duration: ~800 years Status: The Age of Blood

Mareatha's religion proved the most virulent and expansionist. Her arterial baptisms spread like plague, rewriting loyalty at the biological level. Veinspire became the closest thing to a "dominant power."

Key Events:

  • Veinspire Cathedral completes its 12th expansion
  • Arterial Inquisition forms
  • Blood-road infrastructure connects continents
  • Mass conversions (willing and forced)
  • First organized resistance movements form
Cultural Impact:
  • "Pure blood" becomes political currency
  • Heresy redefined as biological contamination
  • Free will debated as medical condition
  • Art flourishes in regions beyond Mareatha's reach
End Condition: Other Sovereigns begin actively countering Mareatha's expansion, creating the current multipolar world.

---

Era VI: The Silent Age

Duration: ~300 years (ongoing in some regions) Status: The Quieting

Azerach's influence spread beyond his desert. Language vanished from entire kingdoms. Communication became gesture, touch, and desperate silence.

Manifestations:

  • The Wordless Kingdoms established
  • Silent monasteries founded
  • New forms of magic dependent on soundlessness
  • Entire generations born unable to speak
  • Music becomes weaponized
Cultural Impact:
  • Philosophy shifts toward the unsaid
  • Truth becomes commodity
  • Listening becomes power
  • Silence worshipped as divine
End Condition: Localized; some regions remain silent to this day.

---

Era VII: The Clockfall

Duration: Impossible to measure (time broke) Status: Temporal Catastrophe

Imhuran's experiments in perfect justice caused time itself to fail. Clocks ran backward. Futures became pasts. People aged centuries in minutes or remained unchanged for millennia.

Manifestations:

  • Causal loops trap entire cities
  • The "Frozen Moment" crystallizes a 10-year span into eternal recurrence
  • Time refugees flee collapsing chronologies
  • Fursen imprisoned for "eternity" (actually ~600 subjective years)
Mortal Response:
  • Temporal cartography developed
  • "Time-blind" prophets emerge
  • Survival becomes predicting next temporal wave
End Condition: Imhuran stabilized his sanctum but left permanent temporal scars across the world.

---

Era VIII: The Age of Rebellion

Duration: ~200 years (current era begins here) Status: The Awakening

Mortals began questioning. Scholars studied the corpse. Theorists mapped organs. Hunters sought fragments. Heretics denied Sovereign divinity.

Major Movements:

  • The Resonant Theorists - Scientists studying the Maker's remains
  • The Organ Hunters - Mercenaries seeking divine fragments
  • The Free Cities Alliance - Nations rejecting Sovereign rule
  • Temporal Rebels - Those escaped from Imhuran's prisons
Key Conflicts:
  • The Vein-Cutter Uprising (failed)
  • The Liberation of Three Cities (partial success)
  • The Silence-Breaker Riots (crushed)
  • The Bone-Architect Schism (ongoing)
Cultural Impact:
  • Hope returns
  • Technology advances
  • Magic becomes experimental rather than devotional
  • Questions replace worship
End Condition: The party's emergence from the 13-Hour Trial.

---

Era IX: The Present Era

Duration: Just beginning Status: THE NOW

The party completed the 13-Hour Trial and shook Veinspire's foundation. Three hearts quieted. Divine attention drawn. Something fundamental shifted.

The Party's Actions:

  • Three hearts quieted - Mareatha's circulatory grip cracking
  • Divine attention drawn - Multiple Sovereigns now aware
  • Kael's anomaly revealed - Vor'Kael's regurgitated fragment walks free
  • Jonas marked - Azerach's brand means the Whispered King is listening
  • Sol Raven linked to dead god - Potential Aedwynn-Born connection
  • Fursen freed - Escaped Imhuran's temporal prison, haunted by lost time
Current State:
  • Veinspire's infrastructure destabilizing
  • Sovereigns reassessing mortal threat levels
  • Rumors of "god-killers" spreading
  • Reality feeling less stable
  • Ancient prophecies referenced
  • Resistance movements emboldened
What Comes Next: Unknown. But the world is waking, and the Sovereigns are watching.

---

Timeline Visualization

``` Era I [Creation] ━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━ Perfection Era II [Murder] ⚡ THE USURPATION ⚡ Era III [Division] ━━━━━━ 2000 years of kingdom-building Era IV [Collapse] ⚠️⚠️⚠️ Reality fractures Era V [Blood] 🩸 Veinspire ascendant Era VI [Silence] 🤫 Language vanishes Era VII [Time] ⏰💥 Chronology breaks Era VIII [Rebellion] ⚔️ Mortals awaken Era IX [NOW] 🔥 The party changes everything ```

---

Timeline Summary

EraNameDurationKey Event
IAedwynn's BreathPre-historyDivine creation
IIThirteenfold MurderOne night/yearsUsurpation
IIIThirteen Kingdoms~1000 yearsSovereign reigns establish
IVShattered Reality~500 yearsGreat Fracture
VVeinspire's Dominion~300 yearsMareatha expands
VISilent Age~200 yearsAzerach's language removal
VIIClockfall~150 yearsTemporal chaos
VIIIAge of Rebellion~100 yearsOrgan Wars begin
IXPresent EraNowParty emerges
Total Known History: ~2,250 years since the Usurpation Before the Usurpation: Unknown, possibly infinite

---

Legendary Events

The Leonin Transgression

The crime that doomed Fursen's people. Details unknown, possibly:
  • Breaking a divine law during Aedwynn's era
  • Attempting to wake the sleeping Maker
  • Stealing something from the proto-Sovereigns
  • Violating sacred boundaries

The First Breath

When Azerach first used the Lungs to remove all sound from a city.

The Bone Ascension

When Vortegas transformed an entire population into living architecture.

The Arterial Schism

When Mareatha's veins rebelled and formed their own consciousness.

The Dream Drowning

When Nhalyra merged 10,000 minds into a single oceanic consciousness.

---

Related

  • The Thirteen Sovereigns
  • The Party
  • Campaign Index - The Ashen Realms
---

"History is a wound that refuses to close. We live in the scar tissue of divine murder, and every generation thinks theirs will be the one to heal it. They're always wrong."
items.raw:
# History & Timeline

The history of the Ashen Realms is measured not in years, but in fractures—moments when reality broke and reformed around new truths. Since the murder of Aedwynn the Maker, time itself has become unreliable, looping in some regions, frozen in others, racing forward in still more.

---

## The Nine Eras

### Era I: The Era of Aedwynn's Breath (Creation)
**Duration:** Unknown (predates measurable time)  
**Status:** Mythical

The beginning. Aedwynn the Maker shaped the world ...

items.title: Major Factions & Organizations
items.content:
Major Factions & Organizations

The world is divided not just by Sovereign domains, but by the ideologies, cults, guilds, and movements that thrive within them. These factions shape politics, religion, trade, and rebellion across the Ashen Realms.

---

SOVEREIGN-ALIGNED FACTIONS

AZERACH THE WHISPERED KING — Factions of Still Air

The Choir of Last Breaths

Type: Monastic archival order Alignment: Devoted to Azerach the Whispered King

A silent monastic order who ritually exhale all sound, storing it in lung-urns. They serve as archivists of forbidden truths inhaled by Azerach.

Goal: Preserve the "Final Breath" of the world before Azerach claims all sound Methods:

  • Silent pilgrimages across the Dominion of Still Air
  • Suffocation rites for initiates
  • Preservation of dying words in ritual containers
Beliefs:
  • All truths end in silence
  • Words decay meaning
  • Breath awakens cosmic predators
Sigil: A ribcage wrapped around a fading whisper

---

The Earglass Sentinels

Type: Warrior order

Warriors with ears lining their armor, able to hear lies as physical vibrations. They serve as enforcers of the Law of Breath, hunting those who speak forbidden words.

---

The Suffocation Envoys

Type: Diplomatic corps

Diplomats who negotiate through controlled asphyxiation. Agreements are sealed when both parties can no longer breathe—ensuring absolute sincerity.

---

IMHURAN THE REGENT OF HOURS — Temporal Factions

The Ledger-Keepers of the Thirteenth Tick

Type: Chronological inquisition Alignment: Devoted to Imhuran the Regent of Hours

Record-keepers who maintain the "Unwritten Ledger," identifying beings who don't belong in the timeline. They hunt temporal anomalies, paradoxes, and existential contradictions.

Notable: Kael's name is still absent from their records, marking him as an ontological error

Methods:

  • Temporal audits
  • Timeline purges
  • Judging those who "skip" their sentences
Goal: Preserve temporal integrity across all realities

---

Chrono-Sentinels

Type: Temporal knights

Knights whose armor is forged from condensed seconds. They move at different speeds than normal time, making them nearly impossible to fight.

---

Delay Priests

Type: Bureaucratic cult

Clerics who weaponize bureaucracy and waiting. They can slow enemies by trapping them in administrative loops.

---

MAREATHA THE VEIN-MOTHER — Blood Factions

The Red Communion

Type: Expansionist blood cult Alignment: Devoted to Mareatha the Vein-Mother

A sect that grafts Mareatha's living capillaries into themselves, spreading "obedience through circulation." They are fanatic evangelists of the arterial faith.

Goal: Expand Mareatha's arterial network into every city Methods:

  • Forced arterial baptisms
  • Infiltration through blood donations
  • Converting entire communities through vascular grafting
Beliefs:
  • Blood determines loyalty
  • Free will is a treatable condition
  • All hearts should beat in synchrony
Power: Operates openly in Sangreal Dominion, covertly elsewhere

---

The Pulse Architects

Type: Biomechanical engineers

Bioengineers who sculpt living cities from vascular tissue. They design the arterial infrastructure of Veinspire's expansion.

---

Vascular Supplicants

Type: Penitent order

Individuals seeking to erase their past through "blood identity replacement"—having their veins replaced with Mareatha's purified circulation.

---

EREDAIN THE CHOIRLESS MONARCH — Resonance Factions

The Gravity Cantors

Type: Sonic warrior-monks Alignment: Devoted to Eredain the Choirless Monarch

Singers trained to bend weight and gravity with emotion-infused song. They believe Eredain's lost celestial chorus can be restored.

Goal: Restore the cosmic song that will "repair" broken gravity Training:

  • Years of emotional conditioning
  • Learning to weaponize sorrow, joy, and rage
  • Singing in inverse-gravity environments
Abilities:
  • Gravity manipulation through song
  • Emotional resonance attacks
  • Levitation choruses
---

The Inverted Choir

Type: Floating monastic order

Monks whose ribs have been surgically modified to function as tuning forks. They float perpetually, humming to maintain local gravity.

---

Lament Knights

Type: Warrior order

Warriors empowered by channeling their own sorrow into gravitational force. The sadder they are, the heavier their strikes.

---

VORTEGAS THE BONE-WARDEN — Ossuary Factions

The Osteal Architects

Type: Bone construction guild Alignment: Devoted to Vortegas the Bone-Warden

Builders who grow bone structures as living cities. They believe all stone should be replaced with bone—the "true" building material.

Goal: Replace every stone structure with ossified architecture Methods:

  • Bone-seed cultivation
  • Marrow irrigation
  • Skeletal scaffolding techniques
Notable Projects:
  • The Marrow Spire
  • Vertebrae Bridge
  • The Ribvault Basilica
---

Marrow Scholars

Type: Academic order

Researchers who consume marrow to gain ancestral memories. Controversial practice that some call cannibalism, others call communion.

---

Skeletal Heralds

Type: Messenger constructs

Messengers made from fused skeletons, animated by Vortegas's will. They deliver judgments across the Ossuary Kingdom.

---

KAEDRIS THE CRIMSON MATRON — Fireblood Factions

The Heartforge Order

Type: Passionate warriors Alignment: Devoted to Kaedris the Crimson Matron

Warriors who open their chests to forge sigils directly onto their living hearts. Each sigil represents a sacred bond or vow.

Goal: Spread passionate devotion—violently if necessary Ritual: The Heartforging

  • Chest is opened surgically
  • Molten iron poured directly onto beating heart
  • Sigil brands the organ
  • Chest sealed with devotional flame
Beliefs:
  • Passion is law
  • Apathy is death
  • Love requires sacrifice
  • Bonds must be tangible
---

Ashen Brides

Type: Priestess order

Women (and some men) whose marriage vows ignite cities. Their ceremonies are literal firestorms of devotion.

---

Ember Children

Type: Street gang / cult

Orphans infused with fireblood by Kaedris's priests. They act as messengers, spies, and arsonists for the Matron's will.

---

ARAL-VYN THE BLADED SAINT — Flesh Ascension Factions

The Hundred-Scalpels Brotherhood

Type: Self-mutilating fanatics Alignment: Devoted to Aral-Vyn the Bladed Saint

Ascetics who remove their own imperfections through ritual cutting. They believe flesh is perfectible through pain.

Goal: Achieve perfect form through surgical excision Practice:

  • Monthly "Corrections" where flaws are cut away
  • Communal surgeries
  • Pain meditation
  • Aesthetic mutilation
Philosophy: "What can be cut away was never truly you"

---

The Nervewalkers

Type: Ascetic order

Monks who walk barefoot on living nerve-pathways, experiencing exquisite agony as spiritual enlightenment.

---

The Skinless Choir

Type: Musical performers

Singers who have shed their skin, believing voices are sharper without flesh to muffle them.

---

VOR'KAEL THE HOLLOW EMPRESS — Hunger Factions

The Cult of the Lost Feast

Type: Philosophical seekers Alignment: Devoted to Vor'Kael the Hollow Empress

Hunters searching for the "First Devouring"—the original concept Vor'Kael consumed that began her endless hunger.

Connection to Kael: They believe Kael is a living remnant of Vor'Kael's first digested idea, making him holy to them

Goal: Find and consume the First Devouring to understand true hunger Methods:

  • Philosophical archaeology
  • Metaphysical excavation
  • Ritual starvation
  • Consuming abstract concepts
---

The Gastric Envoys

Type: Diplomatic order

Diplomats who speak through regurgitated meanings. They vomit up concepts they've consumed, delivering messages in fragmented metaphor.

---

The Hollow Nobles

Type: Aristocratic cult

Emotionless aristocrats who have had their feelings "digested" by Vor'Kael's influence. They desperately seek new emotions to fill the void.

---

ULTHARION THE OBSIDIAN SHEPHERD — Shadow Factions

The Reflection Shepherds

Type: Boundary warriors Alignment: Devoted to Ultharion the Obsidian Shepherd

Warriors who battle their own reflections to stabilize boundary breaches. They guard the line between self and shadow.

Goal: Prevent total boundary collapse Training:

  • Facing mirror-self in combat
  • Learning to trust no reflection
  • Shadow-binding techniques
  • Paranoia meditation
---

The Doubt-Clad Hunters

Type: Predatory order

Assassins who feed on hesitation. They can smell uncertainty and use it to track prey.

---

Mirrorfall Priests

Type: Custodial order

Custodians of bleeding mirrors—reflective surfaces that show traumatic truths. They maintain these dangerous artifacts.

---

ISMARA THE PALE DUCHESS — Thought Plague Factions

The Ivory Quill Ascendancy

Type: Scholarly cult Alignment: Devoted to Ismara the Pale Duchess

Scholars who mutate thoughts intentionally, creating new cognitive structures. They experiment with dangerous ideas.

Goal: Create the "Perfect Idea"—a thought so pure it crystallizes into permanence Methods:

  • Thought grafting
  • Memetic engineering
  • Cognitive surgery
  • Idea cultivation
---

Neuron Wardens

Type: Preservation order

Guardians of crystallized memories, protecting valuable thoughts from decay or theft.

---

Mindrot Collectors

Type: Plague cult

Intentional plague-bearers spreading cognitive decay. They believe intelligence must be pruned like a garden.

---

TALARIS BLOOMREND — Rot & Renewal Factions

The Sporebound Ecclesia

Type: Fungal church Alignment: Devoted to Talaris Bloomrend the Verdant King

Priests who graft fungus into pilgrims, preparing them for the "Great Sprouting"—when all death will bloom into new life.

Goal: Convert the world into a garden of beautiful decay Sacraments:

  • Spore baptism
  • Decomposition meditation
  • Fungal communion
  • Rot blessing
---

Corpse Gardeners

Type: Agricultural order

Farmers who tend to their buried ancestors, cultivating corpse-fruit and flesh-flowers.

---

Rotborn Rangers

Type: Wildlife wardens

Protectors of fungal wildlife, ensuring the cycle of decay continues healthily.

---

NHALYRA THE AZURE QUEEN — Dream & Soulflow Factions

The Drownedsong Choir

Type: Psychic collective Alignment: Devoted to Nhalyra the Azure Queen

Dreamers whose voices ripple through memories and across the Azure Depths. They sing to dissolve individuality.

Goal: Merge all consciousness into a single ocean Practice:

  • Shared dreaming
  • Identity dissolution rituals
  • Soul-osmosis
  • Memory pooling
---

Glasscurrent Navigators

Type: Psychic sailors

Navigators who ride soul currents, guiding ships through the emotional tides of the Azure Depths.

---

Memory Diver Guilds

Type: Professional retrievers

Specialists who retrieve drowned identities and lost memories from the ocean floor.

---

KARTHAYNE THE HOLLOW CROWN — Creation/Breaking Factions

The Crownbreakers

Type: Destructive artists Alignment: Devoted to Karthayne the Hollow Crown

Artisans who destroy their own masterworks to worship Karthayne. They create beauty only to shatter it.

Goal: Break the world beautifully Philosophy:

  • Creation without destruction is stagnation
  • Perfection must be smashed
  • Only through breaking can things improve
Ritual: The Final Canvas—creating a masterpiece, then destroying it at its peak

---

The Ruinwright Cartel

Type: Architectural saboteurs

Architects who design collapsing cities—structures meant to fail spectacularly.

---

Heart-Split Prophets

Type: Oracular order

Seers who speak prophecies in collapsing realities, their words fragmenting across timelines.

---

INDEPENDENT MAJOR FACTIONS

The Veiled Academy

Type: Secret scholarly resistance Alignment: Anti-Sovereign

Hidden network of theorists, rebels, and scholars who study how to resist Sovereign influence and manipulate reality.

Goal: Understand the Maker's death and potentially reverse it Membership: Invitation only Locations: Hidden across all continents

---

The Resonant Union

Type: Reality manipulation guild Alignment: Neutral

Scholars who have learned to manipulate the fundamental frequencies of reality—gravity, time, matter.

Services:

  • Reality consultation
  • Anomaly repair
  • Sovereign interference mitigation
---

Related

  • The Thirteen Sovereigns
  • Criminal Underworld
  • Locations & Realms

items.title: Savaros's Reliquary Items
items.content:
Savaros's Reliquary Items

--- type: item_collection source: "Savaros the High Keeper" location_found: "Catacomb Reliquary, Veinspire" session_acquired: 18 tags: - item - collection - savaros - session-18 ---

Savaros's Reliquary Items

Info
Recovered from the Catacomb Reliquary
Items found at Savaros's altar after his defeat in Session 18.

---

Items Recovered

Heretic's Silence

Wondrous item, rare

A black iron suppression collar with violet runes etched into the metal.

Properties:

  • Wearer cannot cast spells
  • Wearer cannot activate magic items
  • Wearer cannot speak above a whisper
  • Removal requires command word (Savaros knew it) or DC 20 Dexterity (thieves' tools) check
Purpose: Tribunal standard for prisoner transport. Used to suppress heretics during interrogation and transfer.

---

Savaros's Ledger

Wondrous item, unique (no rarity — priceless intelligence)

A book bound in human skin. Fifteen centuries of intelligence records written in handwriting that changes every few pages — different stolen hands recording different secrets.

Contents:

  • Compromised Church officials (names, positions, leverage)
  • Operational details about the Cardinal
  • Dates, locations, and expected attendees for the Confluence
  • Notes on the Seven Heretics (including Mira)
  • Secrets extracted from countless interrogations
  • Internal Church politics and power structures
Value: Incalculable. This is fifteen hundred years of blackmail material, intelligence, and evidence of corruption at the highest levels of Mareatha's Church.

Warning: Possessing this book makes the party targets for everyone mentioned in it — and for Ultharion, who invested fifteen centuries in gathering this information.

---

Truthseeker's Wand

Wondrous item, uncommon (requires attunement)

A rod of dark wood wrapped in copper wire, capped with bone at both ends. The bones are human finger bones — taken from "proven liars," according to Savaros's notes.

Charges: 3 (regains 1d3 at dawn)

CostEffect
1 chargeCast Zone of Truth (DC 15)
2 chargesCast Detect Thoughts (DC 15)
Purpose: Savaros's interrogation tool. Used to extract confessions from heretics and verify truthfulness.

---

Bottled Absence

Wondrous item, rare (single use)

A glass vial containing condensed void — darkness from Ultharion the Obsidian Shepherd's domain. The darkness inside recoils from light and moves when watched.

Effect (when broken):

  • Creates 20-foot radius magical darkness for 1 minute
  • Cannot be dispelled by light spells of 3rd level or lower
  • Inside the darkness: darkvision doesn't work
  • Creatures without darkvision must make DC 14 WIS save or be frightened
Skin-Walker Use: A Skin-Walker can consume the vial's contents to regain 50 HP and gain one additional shapechange.

Origin: A gift from Ultharion — literal bottled darkness from the Obsidian Shepherd's realm.

---

The Confessor's Glass

Wondrous item, very rare (requires attunement)

A silver-framed hand mirror. The frame is made of interlocking hands — a binding gesture. The mirror was found face-down on a pedestal (containment position).

Command Word Activation: Force a creature viewing the mirror to make a DC 16 CHA save:

  • Failed Save: The mirror shows the viewer's true self — their guilt, shame, hidden nature. They take 3d8 psychic damage and must answer one question truthfully.
  • Successful Save: Unease only; immune to this effect for 24 hours.
Special: Always reveals Skin-Walkers regardless of save result. The mirror cannot be fooled by shapeshifting.

Purpose: Savaros's insurance against betrayal. He used it to ensure his agents weren't replaced by rival Skin-Walkers.

Warning: Looking into the mirror yourself can be dangerous. It shows true selves — including aspects you'd rather not see.

---

The Flayer's Touch

Wondrous item, rare (requires attunement)

Black leather gloves with vein-like stitching, lined with preserved human skin.

Passive:

  • Advantage on Sleight of Hand checks
  • Advantage on checks to grapple creatures
Harvest Visage (1/day): Touch a humanoid corpse that has been dead less than 1 hour. Spend 1 minute to remove the face perfectly.
  • The face is preserved indefinitely
  • Can be worn as a mask for advantage on Deception checks to impersonate that person for 8 hours
  • After 8 hours, the face decays and becomes useless
  • Does NOT grant the voice, mannerisms, or memories of the deceased
Purpose: Savaros's original tools — before Ultharion granted him true identity-consumption abilities. These gloves were how he stole his first faces.

Warning: Using these gloves is deeply disturbing. Harvesting a face is a violation that most would consider evil.

---

Also Recovered

Gold: Approximately 1,000 gold pieces stashed at the altar.

---

Items Destroyed (By Jonas)

The following items were on Brother Aldric's corpse and were burned when Jonas threw the body into the brazier:

ItemRarityEffect (Lost)
The First Tear (holy symbol)RareAdvantage vs. fear, emergency healing
The Keeper's Seal (ring)Uncommon→RareSpeak with Dead enhancement, death investigation
Alara's Promise (locket)UncommonMemory protection, death save advantage
Marek's Letter—Evidence of Savaros's infiltration
These items and their properties are permanently lost.

---

Current Holders

ItemHolder
Savaros's LedgerParty inventory
Heretic's SilenceParty inventory
Truthseeker's WandParty inventory
Bottled AbsenceParty inventory
The Confessor's GlassParty inventory
The Flayer's TouchParty inventory
1,000 gold piecesParty inventory
---

Related

  • Savaros the High Keeper
  • The Bleeding Blade
  • Campaign Sessions
  • Ultharion the Obsidian Shepherd

items.title: Species & Cultures
items.content:
Species & Cultures

The Ashen Realms are populated by diverse intelligent species, many shaped or created by the influence of the Thirteen Sovereigns.

---

MAJOR SPECIES

1. Humans

The most widespread species, highly adaptive and found across all Sovereign domains.

Characteristics:

  • Versatile and resilient
  • Short-lived compared to other species (60-80 years baseline)
  • Easily influenced by Sovereign proximity
  • Form the bulk of most civilizations
Cultural Variance: Human cultures vary dramatically based on regional Sovereign influence. Humans in Veinspire are vastly different from those in the Ossuary Kingdom or the Dominion of Still Air.

---

2. Leonin (Fursen's Kind)

Nomadic feline humanoids, nearly extinct due to ancient divine wrath.

Physical Traits:

  • Bipedal feline features
  • Natural predators with enhanced senses
  • Lifespan: 100-150 years
  • Powerful physiques built for survival
History: The Leonin were once a proud warrior culture until an ancestor broke a fundamental taboo—likely involving temporal manipulation or defiance of the natural order. Imhuran the Regent of Hours enacted punishment that nearly destroyed their entire species.

Current Status:

  • Fewer than a thousand remain
  • Scattered across the world, rarely forming communities
  • Many disguise their heritage
  • Fursen is one of the last known Leonin
Cultural Identity: Leonin carry deep ancestral guilt. Many seek redemption, though they don't always know what their ancestors did wrong.

---

3. Aedwynn-Born (God-Touched)

Rare individuals born with dormant fragments of the dead god's essence within them.

Traits:

  • Appear as baseline members of any species
  • Unconsciously resist Sovereign influence
  • Sometimes manifest impossible abilities
  • May experience visions of the Maker's memories
Recognition: Most Aedwynn-Born don't know what they are. They simply feel "different" or experience inexplicable phenomena around them.

Sol Raven Connection: Sol Raven is believed to be Aedwynn-Born, explaining their unusual resistance to divine authority and connection to the deceased Maker.

Rarity: Perhaps 1 in a million births. The Sovereigns hunt them when discovered.

---

4. Resonants

Scholars and monks altered by prolonged exposure to Eredain the Choirless Monarch's sonic domains.

Physical Changes:

  • Hovering gait—they float inches above ground
  • Harmonic speech—their voices produce musical overtones
  • Sensitive to emotional frequencies
  • Glowing throat chakras that pulse with sound
Abilities:
  • Can "hear" emotions
  • Speak in chords that affect gravity
  • Sense structural weaknesses through vibration
  • Navigate by echolocation
Culture: Resonants form meditative communes where silence is considered the highest form of sound. They believe all existence is music waiting to be correctly interpreted.

---

5. Glassbound (Nhalyrian)

Humanoids with liquid-crystal souls, native to Nhalyra the Azure Queen's domains.

Appearance:

  • Translucent skin showing moving patterns beneath
  • Eyes that reflect instead of absorbing light
  • Slow, flowing movements
  • Can partially liquefy when submerged
Nature: Glassbound experience existence differently—their memories are fluid, their identities malleable. They can share consciousness when touching.

Lifespan: Indeterminate. Some Glassbound are thousands of years old, having dissolved and reformed countless times.

Society: They live in underwater cities made of living crystal, communicating through dream-osmosis.

---

6. Marrowkin (Vortegas)

Skeletal humanoids with intelligent bone-structures native to Vortegas the Bone-Warden's realm.

Physical Traits:

  • Living skeletons with minimal soft tissue
  • Bone grows continuously throughout life
  • Can reshape their own skeletal structure
  • Marrow glows faintly at night
Biology: Marrowkin don't have traditional organs. Their marrow performs all biological functions. They "breathe" through porous bone.

Culture:

  • Highly architectural society—they build from their own shed bone
  • Practice ritual bone-shaping as art
  • Believe structure is the only true form of beauty
  • Elders become massive, immobile monuments
Lifespan: 200-300 years, growing heavier and larger until they can no longer move.

---

7. Fungelites (Talaris)

Humanoids born from decomposed corpses, retaining echoes of their former lives.

Creation: When a body decomposes in Talaris Bloomrend the Verdant King's influence, sometimes the fungal growth forms a new consciousness using fragments of the deceased's memory.

Appearance:

  • Mushroom-cap "hair"
  • Bark-like skin with moss patches
  • Spore clouds when emotional
  • Root tendrils instead of toes
Memory: Fungelites have incomplete memories of their "previous life"—enough to feel continuity but not enough to truly be the same person.

Philosophy: They believe death and rebirth are the same process. Grief is treated as compost for new joy.

---

MID-TIER SPECIES

8. Arterials

Humanoids whose blood has been replaced with Mareatha the Vein-Mother's divine ichor.

Traits:

  • Veins visible and glowing beneath skin
  • Can survive without organs as long as blood flows
  • Extremely loyal to Mareatha
  • Blood pressure tied to emotional state

9. Whisperfolk

Lung-mutated silent wanderers created by Azerach the Whispered King's influence.

Traits:

  • Cannot speak—communicate through sign language
  • Hear all sounds within a mile
  • Lungs expanded into secondary chambers
  • Can hold breath for hours

10. Graftlings

Flesh-modified apprentices serving Aral-Vyn the Bladed Saint.

Traits:

  • Bodies augmented with muscle grafts
  • Pain tolerance enhanced
  • Limbs can be replaced and reattached
  • Scars glow when exerting strength

11. Shadebound

Mirror-attuned intelligences connected to Ultharion the Obsidian Shepherd.

Traits:

  • Cast multiple shadows
  • Can "step" into reflections
  • Identity shifts based on observer
  • Fear sunlight

12. Spore-Touched

Fungal symbiotes merged with hosts in Talaris Bloomrend the Verdant King's realm.

Traits:

  • Mushrooms grow from body
  • Regenerate from spores
  • Share thoughts via mycelium network
  • Photosynthesis provides partial nutrition

13. Driftborn

Oceanic dreamers from Nhalyra the Azure Queen's depths.

Traits:

  • Amphibious
  • Dream while awake
  • Memories stored in water
  • Cry crystallized emotions
---

MINOR SPECIES

14. Bonewyrds

Ambulatory skeleton-fragments—individual bones given rudimentary intelligence.

15. Pulse-Gnats

Emotional parasites—tiny creatures that feed on strong feelings.

16. Echo-Imps

Sound-eaters—mischievous beings that consume specific frequencies.

17. Hollow Sprites

Abstract scavengers that feed on abandoned concepts.

18. Skin-Crawlers

Boundary-shifting humanoids who can shed and regrow their outer layers.

---

Species Relations

Conflict:

  • Leonin are distrusted due to ancestral crime
  • Fungelites disturb those who fear death
  • Aedwynn-Born are hunted by Sovereign agents
  • Arterials are seen as Mareatha's infiltrators
Cooperation:
  • Humans and most species coexist in major cities
  • Resonants serve as mediators due to emotional sensitivity
  • Glassbound are sought as archivists and memory-keepers
  • Marrowkin are respected as builders
Taboos:
  • Mixing Arterial blood with non-Arterials (considered heresy)
  • Killing a Fungelite (they might be your ancestor)
  • Enslaving an Aedwynn-Born (attracts divine wrath)
  • Breaking a Marrowkin's bones (equivalent to murder)
---

Related

  • The Thirteen Sovereigns
  • Major Factions & Organizations
  • The Party
items.raw:
# Species & Cultures

The Ashen Realms are populated by diverse intelligent species, many shaped or created by the influence of the Thirteen Sovereigns.

---

## MAJOR SPECIES

### 1. Humans
The most widespread species, highly adaptive and found across all Sovereign domains.

**Characteristics:**
- Versatile and resilient
- Short-lived compared to other species (60-80 years baseline)
- Easily influenced by Sovereign proximity
- Form the bulk of most civilizations

**Cultural Variance:**
Human c...

items.title: The Bleeding Blade
items.content:
The Bleeding Blade

--- type: item rarity: legendary category: weapon subtype: longsword attunement: required cursed: true current_holder: "Jonas" location_found: "Catacomb Reliquary, Veinspire" session_acquired: 18 tags: - item - legendary - weapon - cursed - mareatha ---

The Bleeding Blade

Danger
Legendary Weapon — Cursed
"The Vein-Mother's first gift to her faithful. The blade that taught mortals that sacrifice has power."

Weapon (longsword), legendary (requires attunement) Current Holder: Jonas Acquired: Session 18 — Catacomb Reliquary

---

Description

A longsword of dark iron — not black, but the deep red-brown of dried blood. Veins run through the metal itself, hairline fractures that pulse faintly with crimson light. Like arteries. Like the sword is alive.

The crossguard is shaped like two hands pressed together in prayer, wrists opened, blood flowing down to form the grip. The pommel is a clot — a hardened sphere of something that might be ruby, might be crystallized blood.

It's beautiful. It's obscene.

---

Abilities

Blood Drinker (Passive)

When you deal damage to a creature that has blood, the Bleeding Blade drinks deep. You gain temporary hit points equal to half the damage dealt (rounded down). These temporary HP last for 1 minute and don't stack — new temporary HP replaces old.

Crimson Tithe (Active)

3 charges, regains 1d3 at dawn

As a bonus action, you can sacrifice your own vitality to empower the blade:

CostEffect
1 charge + 10 HPNext attack this turn deals +2d8 necrotic damage
2 charges + 20 HPNext attack deals +4d8 necrotic; target DC 17 CON save or speed halved until end of their next turn
3 charges + 30 HPNext attack deals +6d8 necrotic; target DC 17 CON save or stunned until end of their next turn
This HP sacrifice cannot be reduced or prevented by any means. The blade demands real blood.

---

Curse — The Vein-Mother's Thirst

Danger
Once attuned, the blade hungers.

Daily Hunger: If you finish a long rest without having dealt damage to a living creature with the Bleeding Blade since your last long rest, you take 2d10 necrotic damage that cannot be healed until you feed the blade.

Death's Pull: While attuned, you have disadvantage on death saving throws. Your blood wants to leave your body.

Bound: The blade cannot be unequipped willingly. Breaking attunement requires Remove Curse cast at 7th level or higher, or Mareatha's direct intervention.

---

Dormant Sentience

The Bleeding Blade contains an echo of something — not quite sentient, but aware.

  • It pulses when near large amounts of blood
  • It grows warm in battle
  • It grows cold when sheathed for too long
It remembers Mira. It felt her die.

The blade was in the Catacomb Reliquary when Savaros consumed Mira Alethain. It experienced her death. Whether this has changed the blade in some way remains to be seen.

---

History

The Bleeding Blade is one of Mareatha's oldest relics — created in the early days after the Usurpation when the Vein-Mother's faithful were still learning what divine blood could do.

It was kept in the Catacomb Reliquary for centuries, displayed at the altar as a symbol of sacrifice and devotion. Savaros the High Keeper guarded it — though he served Ultharion, not Mareatha.

Session 18: Jonas took the blade from the altar after Savaros's defeat. It bit him for 5 necrotic damage as introduction — a small tithe to establish the bond.

---

Connection to Mareatha

The blade is infused with the Vein-Mother's essence. While attuned:

  • Servants of Mareatha may recognize the blade's signature
  • The blade may react in proximity to Mareathan holy sites
  • Mareatha herself could theoretically sense the blade if she chose to pay attention
Whether Mareatha would approve of an enemy of her Church wielding her relic... is another question.

---

Related

  • Jonas
  • Mareatha the Vein-Mother
  • Savaros the High Keeper
  • Mira Alethain
  • Catacomb Reliquary
  • Campaign Sessions

items.title: The Border Marches
items.content:
The Border Marches

---

Overview

Info
The Spaces Between
The Border Marches are the transitional zones between Sovereign territories — places where two divine organs meet, their influences bleeding into each other, creating regions of chaos, opportunity, and danger. No Sovereign fully controls a Border March. Most don't want to.

The Marches are home to outlaws, refugees, traders, monsters, and the desperate. They're where the rules break down. Where you can disappear. Where empires end and wilderness begins.

Total Border March territory: Approximately 800,000 square miles (roughly 10% of the Ashen Realms)

---

The Major Border Marches

1. The Rot's Edge

Between: Circulatory Dominion (Mareatha) and The Blooming Rot (Talaris) Width: 50-100 miles Length: ~600 miles Character: Where blood meets fungus

"The trees bleed here. The blood grows mushrooms. The mushrooms grow veins. Don't drink anything."

Geography

The Rot's Edge is a nightmare of competing biologies. Mareatha's arterial architecture extends into the region — blood-roads, vein-bridges, cardiac pumping stations — but Talaris's spores have infected everything. The result is a landscape where:

  • Trees have circulatory systems that bleed when cut
  • Rivers run with a mixture of blood and fungal slurry
  • The ground pulses with a heartbeat that isn't quite regular
  • Buildings from both territories stand half-consumed by the other

Settlements

SettlementPopulationCharacter
Thornhold~45,000Largest free city; trade hub between territories
Clotburg~8,000Former Mareathan outpost, now independent
Sporemire~3,000Fungal village, native-born immune to both influences
The Bleeding Halt~1,200Caravan waystation, changes hands frequently

Hazards

  • Hybrid Diseases: Infections that combine blood corruption and fungal parasitism
  • Vein-Roots: Underground networks that drain blood from travelers
  • Pulse-Blooms: Flowers that explode with pressurized blood when disturbed
  • Loyalty Spores: Talaris's attempt to counter Mareatha — spores that eat devotion
  • The Arterial Infected: Planted creatures with circulatory systems, hunting for fresh blood

Creatures (Unique to This March)

CreatureCRDescription
Blood-Moss Crawler3Centipede-like, drinks blood, spreads spores
Vein-Root Strangler6Plant that grows through circulatory systems
The Pulsing Ones8Hybrid undead — Planted with beating hearts
Arterial Bloom Mother14Bloom Mother infected with Mareatha's blood
Clot Titan17Massive creature of congealed blood and fungus

Factions

  • Thornhold Merchants' Guild: Controls trade, bribes both Sovereigns
  • The Immune: Native-born who resist both influences, fiercely independent
  • Blood Hunters: Mercenaries who track escaped Mareathan converts
  • Spore Runners: Smugglers who transport goods through fungal networks
---

2. The Bone Fields

Between: Ossuary Kingdom (Vortegas) and Bastion of Fireblood (Kaedris) Width: 80-150 miles Length: ~700 miles Character: Where structure meets passion

"The bones burn here. But they don't stop standing."

Geography

An endless plain of exposed skeletal architecture — Vortegas's influence manifests as vast bone formations rising from the earth like frozen waves. But Kaedris's heat permeates everything, causing the bones to glow with internal fire, crack with thermal stress, and occasionally ignite when passions run high nearby.

  • Bone spires that serve as heat vents, glowing orange at night
  • Calcified forests where trees became skeleton and skeleton became charcoal
  • Thermal geysers erupting through rib-cage formations
  • The ground itself is calcium and ash

Settlements

SettlementPopulationCharacter
Calcifer's Rest~35,000Mercenary capital; neutral ground for hiring
Ember Ossuary~12,000Mining town; harvests heat-treated bone
The Char~5,000Refugee camp that became permanent
Kindlespine~2,500Religious community worshipping "the burned god"

Hazards

  • Passion Fires: Emotions (especially anger, love, grief) can cause spontaneous combustion
  • Bone Storms: Thermal winds that carry razor-sharp bone fragments
  • Calcium Leeching: The ground absorbs calcium from living creatures
  • Heat Madness: Prolonged exposure causes emotional instability
  • The Standing Dead: Skeletons that burn eternally but never fall

Creatures (Unique to This March)

CreatureCRDescription
Char Hound2Skeletal wolf wreathed in flame
Bone Salamander5Lives in thermal vents, calcium shell
Passion Wraith7Ghost of someone who burned for love/hate
The Kindled10Vortegas's skeletal constructs with Kaedris's fire
Pyre Colossus16Massive bone giant with molten core

Factions

  • The Ashen Company: Elite mercenaries, take contracts from anyone
  • Bone Traders: Harvest and sell heat-treated skeletal material
  • The Kindled Faithful: Believe the burning bones are a new god being born
  • Firewalkers: Monks who've achieved immunity to passion fires
---

3. The Screaming Corridor

Between: Dominion of Still Air (Azerach) and Shattered Gravitas (Eredain) Width: 100-200 miles Length: ~550 miles Character: Where silence meets sound

"Some days you can't hear yourself think. Other days, thinking is the only sound there is."

Geography

A landscape in constant acoustic warfare. Azerach's silence pushes from one side — zones where sound simply ceases, where your heartbeat becomes the loudest thing in existence. Eredain's resonance pushes from the other — regions where every sound amplifies, where a whisper becomes a scream, where gravity itself responds to vibration.

The border between them shifts constantly, creating:

  • Walls of absolute silence moving across the landscape
  • Pockets of devastating resonance that shatter stone
  • Gravity anomalies triggered by sound
  • Places where speaking causes you to float; places where singing kills

Settlements

SettlementPopulationCharacter
The Quiet Market~20,000Trade city; all business in sign language
Resonance Hold~8,000Built in a stable acoustic pocket
The Whispertown~4,000Only whispers are safe here
Deafhollow~6,000Entire population is deaf (by choice or necessity)

Hazards

  • Sound Zones: Unpredictable regions of silence or amplification
  • Gravity Pockets: Areas where sound affects weight
  • Resonance Cascades: Chain reactions of amplifying sound
  • The Silence: Zones where even thought becomes audible
  • Echo Predators: Creatures that hunt by sound reflection

Creatures (Unique to This March)

CreatureCRDescription
Silence Stalker4Hunts in quiet zones, creates silence around itself
Resonance Wasp3Swarm; individual buzz is harmless, swarm is lethal
Echo Shade6Sound-based incorporeal; exists only as vibration
The Unheard9Creatures from deep silence; seeing them makes no sound
Gravity Singer13Massive creature that controls gravity through song
Sonic Titan18Eredain's escaped experiment; walking resonance disaster

Factions

  • The Silent Traders: Merchants who navigate both zones using sign language
  • Listeners: Spies who survive the corridor by becoming sound
  • The Deaf Alliance: Communities that have adapted by removing hearing
  • Sound Cartographers: Map the shifting acoustic boundaries
---

4. The Dissolving Lands

Between: Devoured Courts (Vor'Kael) and Ivory Vale (Ismara) Width: 70-130 miles Length: ~480 miles Character: Where meaning meets knowledge

"I knew why I came here. Then I knew what I came here for. Then I knew. Then I."

Geography

The most existentially dangerous Border March. Vor'Kael's hunger for meaning clashes with Ismara's accumulation of knowledge, creating a region where:

  • Concepts become unstable (the idea of "direction" may fail)
  • Knowledge physically manifests and can be stolen
  • Understanding something makes it less real
  • Names have weight and power; losing your name loses your self
Travelers often lose track of their purpose, their identity, or their understanding of basic concepts like "forward" or "alive."

Settlements

SettlementPopulationCharacter
Vex's Cradle~15,000Inventor refuge; people who think too much to be consumed
The Unnamed~???Town of people who gave up their names for safety
Purpose~7,000Ironically named; residents cling to obsessive focus
The Library Fortress~3,000Ismaran outpost defending against meaning-loss

Hazards

  • Concept Erosion: Abstract ideas (hope, fear, self) can be weakened or lost
  • Knowledge Crystallization: Thoughts become physical, vulnerable objects
  • Purpose Drain: Forgetting why you're doing something mid-action
  • Name Hunger: Entities that eat identities
  • Meaning Storms: Reality becomes philosophically unstable

Creatures (Unique to This March)

CreatureCRDescription
Thought-Eater3Small; consumes specific memories
Concept Parasite5Attaches to ideas, weakens them
The Purposeless7People who lost all meaning, now predatory
Knowledge Golem11Ismaran construct defending crystallized thoughts
Meaning Void15Living absence; proximity erases concepts
The Hungry Question19Vor'Kael spawn; asks questions that unmake answers

Factions

  • The Focused: Monks who maintain identity through obsessive routines
  • Name-Keepers: Guard names for a fee; identity insurance
  • Thought Harvesters: Sell crystallized knowledge to both territories
  • The Questioned: Cultists who believe meaning should be destroyed
---

5. The Tidal Graves

Between: Azure Depths (Nhalyra) and The Blooming Rot (Talaris) Width: 40-80 miles Length: ~400 miles Character: Where dreams meet decay

"The swamp dreams. The dreams rot. The rot dreams again."

Geography

A vast coastal swamp where Nhalyra's ocean meets Talaris's forest. The water here is neither fully liquid nor fully alive — it's dreaming. The plants are neither fully organic nor fully conscious — they're remembering.

  • Mangrove forests that grow from collective nightmares
  • Tidal pools that show visions of other lives
  • Fog that carries fragments of dissolved identities
  • Islands that exist only when someone believes in them

Settlements

SettlementPopulationCharacter
Ashenmire~28,000Fishing city; harvests dream-crabs and memory-kelp
Drifthollow~6,000Built on floating platforms; moves with tides
The Remembered~2,000Village of people who woke from the dreams
Waking Shore~4,500Last land before the Azure Depths

Hazards

  • Dream Tides: Waves that carry fragments of other consciousnesses
  • Identity Bleed: Prolonged exposure causes memories to mix with others
  • The Remembered Rot: Fungal infections that carry memories of the dead
  • Drowning (both kinds): Physical water and metaphysical dream-submersion
  • Belief Islands: Land that disappears if you doubt it

Creatures (Unique to This March)

CreatureCRDescription
Dream-Crab1Edible; eating causes vivid dreams of crab's memories
Memory Kelp2Grasps, shares traumatic memories on contact
Drowned Planted6Planted that fell into the water; now dream-possessed
Tide Wraith8Ghost dissolved in the water; reforms at each tide
The Dreaming Rot12Talaris's fungal network merged with Nhalyra's dreams
Leviathan Seed17Blooming Rot creature growing into an Azure Depths entity

Factions

  • Dream Fishers: Harvest and sell psychoactive sea life
  • The Awake: Escaped dreamers who fight to stay conscious
  • Tide Walkers: Navigate by reading the dream-currents
  • The Drowned Choir: Worship the merging of rot and dream
---

6. The Arterial Wastes

Between: Circulatory Dominion (Mareatha) and Bastion of Fireblood (Kaedris) Width: 60-120 miles Length: ~520 miles Character: Where blood meets fire

"The rivers here dried to dust centuries ago. But they still flow. You can feel them under your feet — hot, dry, thirsty."

Geography

Once, Mareatha's blood-rivers flowed into Kaedris's volcanic territory. The fire boiled them away. Now the Arterial Wastes are a desert of dried blood — rust-red plains, crystallized hemoglobin formations, and the skeletal remains of arterial architecture, all baking under a sun that never seems to set.

  • Dried blood-riverbeds that crack and shift
  • Crystal formations of solidified plasma
  • Mirages that show what the land looked like when blood still flowed
  • Hot springs that bubble with blood instead of water

Settlements

SettlementPopulationCharacter
Dustheart~18,000Mining city; extracts crystallized blood components
The Fever Fort~9,000Military outpost; guards trade routes
Red Haven~5,000Oasis town; one of the few water sources
Pulse Point~2,000Pilgrimage site; a heart still beats underground

Hazards

  • Dehydration: Extreme; water evaporates rapidly
  • Blood Fever: Disease causing obsession with finding liquid blood
  • Mirage Madness: Visions of the past that can trap minds
  • Crystal Storms: Winds carrying razor-sharp dried blood shards
  • The Thirst: The land itself drains moisture from living things

Creatures (Unique to This March)

CreatureCRDescription
Dustblood Scorpion2Drinks blood, stores it in crystallized form
Fever Phantom5Illusion of a blood river; leads travelers to death
Dried Vein Serpent7Desiccated arterial construct; still hunting
The Thirsty10Former travelers consumed by the land's hunger
Bloodstone Elemental14Animated crystallized blood; territorial
The Pulse Below18Whatever still beats beneath Pulse Point

Factions

  • Crystal Harvesters: Mine dried blood for alchemical components
  • The Pulse Pilgrims: Believe the heartbeat can resurrect the rivers
  • Fever Doctors: Treat the blood fever; often have it themselves
  • Wasteland Rangers: Guide caravans; know the safe paths
---

7. The Flickering March

Between: Clockwork Sanctum (Imhuran) and The Blackfold (Ultharion) Width: 90-160 miles Length: ~580 miles Character: Where time meets shadow

"I saw my shadow walk away three days before I arrived. Or after. The tenses don't work here."

Geography

A landscape where time and identity are unreliable. Imhuran's temporal distortions meet Ultharion's shadow corruption, creating regions where:

  • Your shadow arrives before you (or after you leave)
  • Time moves differently for different people standing next to each other
  • Reflections show the past or future (never the present)
  • Shadows can step out of time entirely

Settlements

SettlementPopulationCharacter
Somewhere~12,000City exists in multiple timestreams simultaneously
Shadowfall~7,000Built where shadows pool; stable shadow-time
The Eternal Moment~4,000Frozen in a single instant; residents don't age
Flicker Town~3,500Appears and disappears; residents adapted

Hazards

  • Temporal Desync: Party members experiencing different time rates
  • Shadow Separation: Your shadow becomes independent and hostile
  • Time Loops: Regions that repeat infinitely
  • Age Storms: Pockets of accelerated or reversed aging
  • The Flicker: Reality stuttering, causing injury through temporal shear

Creatures (Unique to This March)

CreatureCRDescription
Time Shadow4Shadow that exists in the past; attacks before you see it
Flicker Beast6Predator that phases between moments
The Desynchronized8People split across multiple timestreams
Temporal Shade11Shadow creature that steals minutes from victims
Yesterday's Monster14Creature from a past that no longer happened
The Loop Keeper17Entity that maintains and feeds on time loops

Factions

  • Chrono-Anchors: People who stabilize time around themselves
  • Shadow Traders: Deal in separated shadows (valuable, dangerous)
  • The Persistent: Refuse to acknowledge time's instability
  • Loop Breakers: Mercenaries who rescue people from temporal prisons
---

Lesser Border Marches

These smaller transitional zones exist but are less traveled:

MarchBetweenCharacter
The Wound's EdgeVerdant Pass / MultipleArea around Talaris's scar
The Breathless ReachDominion of Still Air / Ivory ValeSilence meets thought
The Hungry DarkDevoured Courts / The BlackfoldMeaning and shadow
The Broken StairsShattered Gravitas / Ossuary KingdomGravity and structure
The Drowning SilenceAzure Depths / Dominion of Still AirDreams and quiet
The Flesh FrontierCrucible of Flesh / MultiplePain spreads outward
The Paradox WastesThe Absent Throne / All AdjacentReality fails
---

Travel Through the Marches

General Guidelines

FactorImpact
SpeedHalf normal (terrain difficulty, hazards)
EncountersDouble frequency (lawless, monster-rich)
SuppliesTriple cost (scarcity, danger premium)
GuidesEssential (local knowledge required)
NavigationDifficult (DC 15-20 Survival daily)

Random Event Table (Any March)

d20Event
1-3Hazard specific to this March (save or consequence)
4-5Bandits/refugees (1d6+2 desperate people)
6-7Merchant caravan (trading opportunity)
8-9Creature encounter (roll on March bestiary)
10-11Reality instability (minor, weird but harmless)
12-13Abandoned camp (supplies? trap? bodies?)
14-15Territorial marking (one Sovereign's influence stronger here)
16-17Natural landmark (navigation bonus or shelter)
18-19Travelers (information, rumors, possible allies)
20Major event (Sovereign activity, monster migration, reality breach)
---

Related

  • World Scale Reference
  • Free Cities of the Ashen Realms
  • The Thirteen Sovereigns
  • History & Timeline
---

"The Marches are where the gods couldn't agree. Where their stolen flesh meets and argues. We live in the argument. It's dangerous, but at least no one owns us." — Thornhold merchant

items.title: The Martyr's Gauntlet
items.content:
The Martyr's Gauntlet

---

Overview

Info
The Truth-Seeker's Hand
Type: Legendary Relic
Also Known As: The Fist of Aldric, The Truth-Seeker's Hand
Origin: Aldric Vane, Founder of the Tribunal
Current Location: Unknown — officially in Cathedral vault, actually missing
First Mentioned: Session 18

---

Quick Reference

AttributeValue
TypeGauntlet (relic)
OriginFirst Inquisitor's preserved hand
Official LocationGrand Cathedral vault, Veinspire
Actual LocationUnknown — pedestal empty for decades/centuries
Primary PowerReveals all lies and illusions
---

Legend

The First Inquisitor

"There's a story about a gauntlet forged from the first Inquisitor's bones. The man who founded the Tribunal — before it became what it is now. They say he was a true believer. Actually wanted to help people."
> "When he died, his followers made his hand into a weapon. Wore it to carry on his work."

Powers (Legendary)

"The gauntlet supposedly protects the wearer from lies — you can't be deceived while wearing it. Illusions fail. Disguises unravel."

---

Current Status

The Empty Pedestal

From Seraphine Vale (Session 18):

"I saw the vault. Once. Three years ago. I was part of a ceremonial guard during the Feast of Sacred Blood — one of the few times they open the inner sanctum to anyone below Cardinal rank."
> "The pedestal where the Gauntlet is supposed to rest? Empty. Has been for... I don't know how long. Decades. Maybe centuries."
> "The priests who guard the vault don't even acknowledge it. They walk past the empty pedestal like it's not there. Like they've been trained not to see it."

Theories

From Seraphine and Garrett the Smith:

1. Lost during the Heretic Wars (400 years ago) - Someone stole it - Church pretends it's still there to maintain authority

2. Savaros has it - Moved to the Catacomb Reliquary for "safekeeping" - Some say he wears it - Would explain why he never shows himself — too many lies to risk exposure

3. It walked away on its own (Heresy) - Relics of true faith can't abide what the Tribunal became - Waiting somewhere for a worthy bearer

---

Warning

From Seraphine Vale:

"Be careful what you wish for."
> "A gauntlet that reveals all lies doesn't discriminate. It'll show you the lies others tell."
> "It'll also show you the ones you tell yourself."

---

Potential Mechanics

If found, the Martyr's Gauntlet might provide:

EffectDescription
Truth SightImmune to illusions; see through disguises automatically
Lie DetectionKnow when someone lies within 30 feet
Self-RevelationWearer cannot deceive themselves; disadvantage on self-deception
Curse?The gauntlet may judge the wearer; those unworthy may suffer consequences
---

Connection to Current Events

The Catacomb Reliquary

If Savaros has the Gauntlet, investigating the Reliquary might reveal:

  • The Gauntlet's location
  • Why Savaros hides himself
  • What lies the High Keeper is protecting

The Party's Interest

Jonas asked about the Gauntlet specifically — it could be a valuable tool against:

  • Tribunal deception
  • Sovereign manipulation
  • The Cardinal's web of lies
---

Related

  • Savaros
  • Garrett the Smith
  • Seraphine Vale
  • Catacomb Reliquary
  • Veinspire Cathedral
  • Jonas
---

"A gauntlet that reveals all lies doesn't discriminate. It'll show you the lies others tell. It'll also show you the ones you tell yourself." — Seraphine Vale

items.title: The Realms Beyond
items.content:
The Realms Beyond — Dimensions, Heights, and Depths

---

Overview

Info
The World Is Not Flat
The Ashen Realms are built from a god's corpse — and gods are not two-dimensional. Beyond the surface territories of the Thirteen Sovereigns exist realms above, below, between, and elsewhere. Some are extensions of Sovereign power; others predate the Usurpation; still others were created by the violence of divine murder itself.

---

The Vertical World

The Skyborne Realms

Above the surface, where the atmosphere thins and the corpse-geography ends, other places exist:

The Breath Remnants

Location: Upper atmosphere, primarily above the Dominion of Still Air Nature: Fragments of Aedwynn's exhaled breath, crystallized into floating landmasses

When Aedwynn died, the Maker's last breath escaped — but didn't dissipate. It crystallized in the upper atmosphere, forming floating islands of solidified air. These Breath Remnants drift slowly across the sky, occasionally visible from the ground as pale clouds that cast no shadow.

RemnantCharacterInhabitants
The Final ExhaleLargest fragment; city-sizedMonks of silence; Azerach's observers
The Whisper IslesChain of smaller fragmentsTraders; sky-pirates
The SighSingle massive platformNeutral ground; diplomatic meetings
The CoughUnstable; breaks apart and reformsNo permanent population
Access: Flying creatures, teleportation, or climbing the Listening Peaks during certain atmospheric conditions.

The Resonance Shells

Location: Various altitudes above the Shattered Gravitas Nature: Spherical zones of stable gravity floating in the sky

Eredain's gravity manipulation created stable bubbles in the sky — spherical zones where gravity points inward, allowing habitation on the inner surface. These Resonance Shells range from house-sized to city-sized.

ShellSizeCharacter
The First Harmony3 miles diameterEredain's private retreat
The Floating CourtsVariousNobility; perfect emotional control required
The Exile ShellsSmallPrisons for the discordant
The Wild ShellsVariousUncontrolled; monsters; treasure
Access: Resonance Birds (trained flying creatures), gravity manipulation, or being thrown upward with enough force.

The Dream Canopy

Location: Above the Azure Depths Nature: Where evaporated consciousness collects

The Azure Ocean doesn't just exist as liquid — it evaporates. But soul-infused water becomes soul-infused vapor, rising to form clouds of collective dreaming above the Depths. The Dream Canopy is where the dreams of millions collect, mix, and occasionally take form.

FormationCharacter
The Nightmare BanksDark clouds of terror
The Wish WispsBright formations of desire
The Memory FogDense areas of collective recall
The Lucid PeaksRare stable areas; can be inhabited
Access: Dream Ships with specialized sails, flying while dreaming, or dying in the Azure Ocean (your consciousness rises).

---

The Depths Below

Beneath the surface, in the spaces within and below the god's corpse:

The Visceral Caverns

Location: Throughout the Ashen Realms, beneath all territories Nature: The internal spaces of Aedwynn's body — veins, organs, cavities

The Maker's body is not solid. Beneath the surface territories lie the internal spaces — blood vessels large enough to sail through, organ cavities the size of cities, and passages that connect distant territories through the divine anatomy.

SystemCharacterConnects
The Great VeinsBlood vessel tunnelsCirculatory Dominion to all territories
The Lymphatic WaysImmune system passagesHidden; defensive creatures
The Nerve ChannelsNeural pathwaysIvory Vale to everywhere
The Bone MarrowInside major bonesOssuary Kingdom depths
Access: Finding an opening (wounds, surgical incisions from the Usurpation), traveling through Mareatha's blood-road system, or being swallowed by something large.

The Gut Labyrinth

Location: Beneath the Devoured Courts Nature: The actual digestive system — still functioning

Vor'Kael claimed the Digestive Engine, but she didn't create it. Beneath her Courts lies the actual digestive system of the Maker — miles of intestinal passages, stomach chambers filled with ancient acids, and the sphincter gates that control passage between sections.

SectionCharacter
The Upper StomachPartially accessible; acidic lakes
The Intestinal MilesHundreds of miles of winding passage
The Absorption ZonesWhere meaning is actually extracted
The Lower ReachesWhere waste collects; ancient horrors
Access: The Mouth of the World in Consumption, hidden passages throughout the Courts, or being consumed and surviving.

The Marrow Kingdoms

Location: Inside the major bones, beneath the Ossuary Kingdom Nature: Civilizations within the skeletal structure

Vortegas rules the bones from their surface, but within the bones — in the marrow cavities — other societies exist. Some are loyal to the Bone-Warden; others predate his rule; still others are in active rebellion.

KingdomCharacter
The Marrow LordsVortegas's underground governors
The Calcium RepublicsIndependent city-states
The Narrow FolkNatives who've never seen the surface
The Rebellion CellsThose who reject Sovereign rule
Access: Tithe Vaults lead downward, certain bone formations have natural openings, Bone Architects can create passages.

The Sunken Dream

Location: Beneath the Azure Ocean's floor Nature: Where the heaviest consciousness settles

Below the Undertow Deeps, below even the ocean floor, lies the Sunken Dream — the absolute bottom where the densest, oldest, most concentrated consciousness has pooled since before the Usurpation. Things live here that remember being part of Aedwynn. Things that want to reassemble.

LayerCharacter
The Sediment of SelvesCompressed personalities
The Primal MemoriesPre-mortal recollections
The Dreaming CorePossibly Aedwynn's own dreams
The Waking TitanSomething that might be stirring
Access: Diving beyond safe limits, dying in the Depths and sinking, or finding the passages that lead down from the Gut Labyrinth.

---

The Spaces Between

Not above, not below — elsewhere:

The Wound Dimensions

Location: At every place the Maker was cut during the Usurpation Nature: Pocket dimensions created by divine injury

When the Thirteen cut Aedwynn apart, each wound didn't just bleed — it tore. The Wound Dimensions are pocket realms that exist in the space of these tears, accessible from the scars left behind.

WoundLocationCharacter
The Lung TearAzerach's territory borderScreaming void; wind that never stops
The Heart WoundBeneath VeinspireBleeding dimension; endless crimson
The Brain IncisionIvory Vale depthsThoughtscape; pure concept
The Gut OpeningDevoured CourtsHunger given form
The Skin FlayingBlackfold bordersRaw exposure; no boundaries
Access: Finding the original wound sites (often hidden or guarded), carrying a weapon that wounded the Maker, or being of the same bloodline as the Thirteen.

The Reflection Realm

Location: Behind every mirror in the Blackfold (and spreading) Nature: The space where reflections live

Ultharion's domain includes not just shadows but reflections. Behind every reflective surface in the Blackfold lies the Reflection Realm — a parallel space where mirror-selves exist, living reversed lives, plotting to trade places with their originals.

ZoneCharacter
The Mirror CourtsWhere reflection nobility rules
The Reversed CitiesBackward versions of real places
The Glass PrisonWhere reflections trap originals
The True ReflectionWhere you see yourself as you really are
Access: Step through a Mirror Lake, be pulled in by your reflection, or find a mirror that's cracked in the right way.

The Paradox Depths

Location: Where the Absent Throne intersects with reality Nature: Dimensions of pure contradiction

The Absent Throne doesn't just exist in one place — it bleeds into everywhere, creating pocket dimensions of paradox wherever reality is weakest. These Paradox Depths are places where the rules of existence don't apply.

DepthCharacter
The Maybe SpaceThings exist contingently
The Never WasHistory that didn't happen made manifest
The Always WillFutures that must occur
The Cannot BeImpossibilities given form
Access: Find a paradox (any logical contradiction), step through during a reality fluctuation, or be sent there by Karthayne (intentionally or not).

The Clockwork Dimensions

Location: Within Imhuran's temporal machinery Nature: Pocket dimensions for each moment of time

The Clockwork Sanctum's machinery doesn't just regulate time — it stores it. Every moment that passes is captured in a dimensional pocket, accessible (with the right keys) for revisiting, alteration, or imprisonment.

DimensionCharacter
The Moment VaultsStored instants; can be entered
The Erased TimelineMoments that were removed
The Parallel TracksAlternate histories
The End of TimeWhere all moments eventually go
Access: Temporal keys (rare artifacts), Imhuran's permission, or being caught in a time loop that intersects with a vault.

---

The Burning Above

Location: Above the Bastion of Fireblood Nature: Where passion becomes weather

The most intense emotions in the Bastion don't just cause fires — they rise, ascending into an atmospheric layer of pure passion-energy. The Burning Above is a realm of emotional weather, where feelings are visible as aurora-like phenomena and can be harvested by those brave enough to fly there.

PhenomenonCharacter
Rage StormsRed lightning; destroys what it touches
Love HazesPink mists; causes bonding
Grief RainsBlue precipitation; induces sorrow
Joy BurstsGolden explosions; temporary euphoria
Access: Ride the thermals from a Passion Peak eruption, use fire magic to ascend, or be thrown there by extreme emotional outburst.

---

The Fleshscape Interior

Location: Within the Crucible of Flesh Nature: The internal anatomy of the Maker's hands

Aral-Vyn rules the surface of the Hands, but within them lie tendons, muscles, nerves, and blood vessels — a landscape of internal anatomy where some communities have lived since before the Usurpation.

LayerCharacter
The Tendon TunnelsMajor passages; trade routes
The Muscle CavernsLarge spaces; still flex occasionally
The Nerve NetworksCarries sensation; dangerous
The Blood PoolsAncient, stagnant; things live there
Access: Refinement procedures that go too deep, finding natural openings at the finger joints, or being absorbed during a muscle contraction.

---

The Dead God's Dreams

The Remnant Consciousness

Location: Uncertain; possibly everywhere Nature: Whatever remains of Aedwynn's mind

The Maker is dead. The Thirteen murdered and dismembered a god. But consciousness that vast doesn't simply end — it fragments, disperses, and possibly continues in some form. The Remnant Consciousness is the hypothetical surviving awareness of Aedwynn, scattered across the world that was once its body.

Some scholars believe:

  • Every dream in the Azure Depths contains fragments of divine thought
  • The Ivory Vale's crystallized knowledge includes the Maker's memories
  • The Absent Throne exists because Aedwynn imagined it as a final act
  • Sol Raven's shard of connection suggests the Maker is not entirely gone
If the Remnant Consciousness exists, it would be:
  • Fragmented across all territories
  • Possibly unaware of itself
  • Theoretically capable of reassembly
  • The most dangerous thing in existence if awakened
---

Travel Between Realms

Known Passages

FromToMethod
SurfaceBreath RemnantsListening Peaks ascent
SurfaceVisceral CavernsBlood-road deep stations
BlackfoldReflection RealmMirror Lakes
Devoured CourtsGut LabyrinthMouth of the World
Clockwork SanctumMoment VaultsTemporal keys
Any wound siteWound DimensionsBlood of the Thirteen
Azure DepthsDream CanopySoul evaporation
Ossuary KingdomMarrow KingdomsTithe Vault descent

Universal Methods

  • Divine Blood: Carrying blood from a Sovereign can open passages
  • Wound Walking: Finding Usurpation wound sites
  • Death and Return: Dying in specific ways grants access to specific realms
  • Sovereign Permission: Any Sovereign can grant access to their realm's depths
---

Related

  • World Scale Reference
  • The Thirteen Sovereigns
  • Aedwynn the Maker
---

"You think you know the Ashen Realms because you've walked their surface? You've seen the skin of a corpse. Beneath that skin are organs, vessels, bones, and marrow. Above it are the last gasps of divine breath. And somewhere in all of it, something that was once a god might still be dreaming." — Scholar of the Deep Places
items.raw:

## Overview

> [!info] The World Is Not Flat
> The Ashen Realms are built from a god's corpse — and gods are not two-dimensional. Beyond the surface territories of the Thirteen Sovereigns exist realms above, below, between, and *elsewhere*. Some are extensions of Sovereign power; others predate the Usurpation; still others were created by the violence of divine murder itself.

---

## The Vertical World

### The Skyborne Realms

Above t...

items.title: The Resonant Codex
items.content:
The Resonant Codex (Transformed)

--- type: item rarity: rare category: wondrous_item attunement: required current_holder: "Sol Raven" original_holder: "Kael" transformation_session: 18 tags: - item - rare - wondrous-item - codex - kael - transformed ---

The Resonant Codex

Info
Transformed by Loss
"The book likes you. I can feel it. Something about what you carry — the Witness thing. It resonates."
— Kael's final entry

Wondrous Item, rare (requires attunement) Current Holder: Sol Raven Original Holder: Kael (deceased) Transformed: Session 18 — Catacomb Reliquary

---

Description

A leather-bound tome of unusual weight. The cover is embossed with symbols that shift when not observed directly. The pages reorganize themselves based on the reader's needs, bringing relevant information forward.

The pages are blood-stained — Kael's blood, from when Savaros consumed him.

---

History

Original Acquisition (Session 3)

The party stole the Resonant Codex from the Veinspire church archives at the request of the Resonance Theorists. Kael became its primary user, studying its contents about pre-Sovereign resonance theory.

Kael's Use

Throughout Sessions 3-18, Kael consulted the Codex regularly. It responded to his nature as an ontological anomaly — a concept that refused dissolution. The book and Kael developed a connection.

Transformation (Session 18)

When Savaros consumed Kael in the Catacomb Reliquary, his blood soaked the Codex's pages. Sol Raven retrieved the book from beside Kael's skeleton.

When Sol touched it, he experienced a vision of Kael's journey — consumption by Vor'Kael, resistance, expulsion. The pressure of a god trying to digest him.

The Codex transformed. It now responds to Sol's nature as the Maker's Witness.

---

Properties

Base Properties (Unchanged)

  • Contains resonance theory and pre-Sovereign knowledge
  • Pages reorganize based on reader's needs
  • Detects ontological anomalies (proximity sensing)
  • Can identify beings that "shouldn't exist"

New Properties (Post-Transformation)

Witness Resonance: Sol's Witness visions are automatically recorded in the Codex. When he accesses the Maker's memory or experiences divine revelation, the book writes it down — preserving the knowledge even if Sol forgets.

Kael's Echo (1/Long Rest): Sol can ask a question about identity, existence, or self. The book answers in Kael's voice.

---

Kael's Echo

Warning
Not a Spirit — An Impression

This is not Kael's soul or consciousness. It is an echo — an impression left in the Codex by a concept that refused to dissolve.

When Sol asks a question:

  • The answer comes in Kael's voice
  • Always honest
  • Often uncertain
  • Occasionally profound
  • Sometimes asks questions back
Limitations:
  • Can only answer questions about identity, existence, or self
  • Does not know current events (stuck in a permanent present)
  • Does not know Kael is dead
  • Cannot take actions or provide tactical information
  • Is not aware of conversations it isn't directly asked into
Sample Interactions:

Sol: "Kael, what does it mean to be real?"
Echo: "I think... it means someone remembers you. It means you left a mark. It means something changed because you existed. Does that count? I hope that counts."

Sol: "Do you remember dying?"
Echo: "Dying? I don't... no. Why would I remember that? Sol, is something wrong?"

---

Kael's Final Entry

Found when Sol first opened the transformed Codex:

"If you're reading this, I'm probably dead. The codex knows things I don't. Sol — if it's you — I'm sorry I couldn't be more useful. I'm sorry I didn't understand what I was in time to matter. But you called me real. You said I was making choices NOW. That it mattered. I hope you were right.
> — Kael
> P.S. — The book likes you. I can feel it. Something about what you carry — the Witness thing. It resonates. Take care of it. Take care of them."

---

Connection to the Witness

The Codex and Sol's Witness abilities have formed a symbiotic relationship:

  • Sol's visions are recorded automatically
  • The Codex organizes divine knowledge for easier access
  • Kael's echo may have insights about Sol's nature as a "wrong thought"
  • Both Sol and the Codex carry pieces of beings that shouldn't exist
---

Related

  • Sol Raven
  • Kael
  • Vor'Kael the Hollow Empress
  • The Resonance Theorists
  • Campaign Sessions
