SESSION 18 — CORRECTION: SAVAROS'S TRUE CAUSE OF DEATH

Important
How Savaros Actually Died
The previous entries incorrectly state that the party "overwhelmed" Savaros through combined assault. This has been corrected.

The True Sequence of Events

1. Savaros consumed Mira Alethain's flesh and skin — ripping it from her bones, reducing her to a skeleton 2. Savaros consumed Kael's flesh and skin — tearing it from his body, leaving a skeleton with a cracked ribcage 3. Savaros began digesting Kael's flesh — and that's when everything went wrong

Why Savaros Died

Kael was an ontological anomaly — a concept that Vor'Kael the Hollow Empress herself had tried to digest and couldn't. She vomited him out because his very nature was indigestible, incompatible with consumption.

When Savaros tried to digest Kael's flesh, the same thing happened to him.

He began to convulse.

The stolen faces beneath his robes contorted in agony. His paper-tearing voice became a shriek of a hundred identities screaming at once. His form shuddered, spasmed, and began to unravel — not from the party's assault, but from the poison he had willingly consumed.

Kael's flesh was toxic to any entity that tried to incorporate it. What Vor'Kael — a Sovereign — couldn't stomach, a mere Skin-Walker had no hope of surviving.

His robes collapsed around emptiness. His mask — Brother Aldric's death mask — clattered to the stone.

Poetic Justice

Ended by consuming something that refused to be consumed.

The concept that wouldn't die... killed the monster that tried to eat it.

Kael — who spent his short existence wondering if he was real, if he mattered, if his choices meant anything — saved the party in death. His very nature, the thing that made him an impossibility, became the weapon that destroyed a fifteen-century-old monster.

He was real enough to kill a Skin-Walker.

---

SESSION 19 — THE VERDANT KING'S DOMAIN

The Journey Begins

Day 1 — Sporehaven to Verdant Pass

The party departed Sporehaven at dawn, carrying Mira's remains in a barrel. The elder woman's warning echoed: "If the King speaks to you — answer honestly. He already knows the truth. Lies just make him hungry."

Party Status:

  • Jonas — The Bleeding Blade fed (rabbit), Azerach's brand "stands out like stone in compost"
  • Sol Raven — Carries the Resonant Codex and Mira's wedding ring
  • Fursen — Carries Mira's remains, checked the Ledger of Moments

The Ledger of Moments — New Entries

Fursen examined Imhuran's self-updating ledger and discovered:

Temporal Prison Status:

  • Cell 2-V: OCCUPIED (dormant)
  • Cell 3-V: OCCUPIED (dormant)
  • Cell 5-V: OCCUPIED (dormant)
  • Cell 7-V: VACANT — Fursen's former cell
  • Cell 9-V: OCCUPIED (dormant)
  • Cell 12-V: STATUS UNCLEAR — temporal echo detected
Four other Leonin remain in temporal containment. Fursen is not the only survivor.

Additional Entry:

"Query from the Obsidian Shepherd regarding Leonin historical records. Request denied. The extinction is complete. There is nothing to discuss. Ultharion probes where he should not. His interest is noted."

The Sweetwater Marsh

Hazard Encountered: The Sweet Springs — crystal-clear pools that compel creatures to drink (DC 14 WIS save). The water promises rest, peace, an end to carrying weight.

Sol Raven Failed (WIS 3): Sol stepped off the Root Path and knelt at the water's edge. Despite being a skeleton who cannot drink, he desperately tried to scoop water with hands that couldn't hold anything. The wanting wasn't in his body — it was somewhere else.

Vines began creeping toward his kneecaps. A Dreaming Stone nearby — a calcified pilgrim who drank and never left — wheezed: "...it doesn't let go..."

Jonas's Intervention: Jonas charged off the path and cut the vines with the Bleeding Blade. The vines shrieked — a vibration felt in the teeth — and retreated. The party snapped Sol out of the compulsion and continued.

The Pilgrim's Spine

Bloom Hound Encounter: Five Bloom Hounds — wolves with flowering vines growing from their bodies — followed the party along the elevated ridge path. They were curious, not aggressive.

The Party Used Burning Hands.

The fire bloomed in the shadows. The hounds didn't have time to yelp. The smell was immediate: charred meat, burned vegetation.

The Forest's Response: The Lantern Posts flickered from green to angry red. The trees groaned — not wind, but displeasure. Fire is anathema here. The old enemy. The army-memory from three centuries ago.

A Choir Bloom appeared on a Watcher's Platform. Jonas approached it.

Jonas Lied: When asked why they brought fire, Jonas claimed the hounds "attacked" and they were "defending themselves."

The Choir Bloom's spores tasted his thoughts. Found the lie.

"The hounds followed because they were curious. You burned them because fire was easy. The King already knows the truth. Lies make him hungry."

"The cycle does not forget, fire-bringer. It only waits."

The Hunger Garden

The party navigated the Bone Trail — previous pilgrims' remains marking the "safe" path. They passed Planted (dead pilgrims reclaimed by the forest), avoided Maw of the Garden pit traps, and witnessed the feeding ecosystem of Talaris's digestive system.

The party's stealth failed (group check: 9). A Thornback Behemoth — a mountain of matted fur, bark-like hide, and massive thorns — noticed them.

Combat:

  • Sol used Immolation and Scorching Ray
  • Jonas used Green Flame Blade with the Bleeding Blade
  • The Behemoth burned. Its regeneration stopped. It fell.
Talaris Spoke Directly: The bioluminescence pulsed outward from the corpse like a shockwave. The fungi screamed. Then a voice emerged from everywhere — from the wood itself:

"Fire-bringers. Burners. You walk the Pilgrim's Path and you bring the old enemy into my body."

"The Grove awaits. We will speak there."

"Walk quickly. My patience thins."

The Congregation Grove

Thirteen massive trees — each over 200 feet tall — formed a ring around a clearing. The air was thick with spores that think. Seven Choir Blooms emerged to conduct The Questioning.

The Four Questions:

1. "What do you carry into the Verdant King's domain?"

  • Fursen: "I carry with me the remains of a very dear friend."
  • Jonas: "I carry the Bleeding Blade."
  • Sol: Listed inventory, then added: "I carry the weight of my fallen comrades as well as the will of the god itself."
The Choir Blooms noted Sol's connection to Aedwynn — "The Maker's Witness. You are a wrong thought that refuses to stop being thought."

2. "What do you seek at the Wound?"

  • "We seek the remains of our fallen comrade's husband."
The Grove acknowledged Elias Alethain — the healer who crawled into the Wound 22 years ago and refused to rot. The King respected his choice.

3. "What have you done in the Verdant King's domain?"

  • Jonas: "I have lied and burned, but I am not exactly seeking violence. It just seems to come to us."
The Choir Bloom was disappointed but not surprised:
"Violence did not find you, fire-bringer. You chose it. Twice. Then lied about the first, and now soften the second."

4. "What would you offer for passage to the Wound?"

The Grove listed acceptable offerings: blood freely given, memory surrendered, service promised, truth confessed, or... Mira's bones surrendered to the cycle.

Sol's Offering — The Maker's Memory

Sol Raven stepped forward and offered something unprecedented:

💀 "I carry the Maker's memory. The world before the Usurpation. Before the Thirteen tore her apart and built their kingdoms on her corpse."

💀 "I can show you."

He activated Shared Memory — extending his skeletal hand to the lead Choir Bloom.

The connection opened. Aedwynn's vision poured through — not just to the Choir Bloom, but through the spore network, through the roots, through the fungal web that connects every living thing in the Blooming Rot.

Talaris Bloomrend saw.

---

THE MAKER'S MEMORY

Light. Not sunlight — something older. The light of a god dreaming the world into being.

Unity. A world without borders. Life and death flowing together as partners in an endless dance.

Her. Aedwynn. The Maker. Not a corpse — alive. Vast beyond comprehension, but also intimate. A mother's hands shaping clay. A physician's touch healing wounds.

She looked at them. Through Sol's memory, through two thousand years of death — she saw.

And her eyes held no anger. Only sorrow.

"My children. What have you done to yourselves?"

---

The Choir Blooms wept — clouds of luminescent gold spores pouring from their caps. The Thirteen Trees groaned overhead like mourning.

"She was... beautiful."

"The King is... the King requires a moment."

Somewhere deep in the forest — miles away, at the Throne of Roots — something ancient was remembering what it used to be. Before it became a monster. Before it helped murder a god.

"The offering is... accepted."

"Pass to the Wound. Complete your task."

"The fire is... the King will consider the fire in light of what you have given. This changes nothing. But it changes... everything."

Jonas's Question — Talaris and Azerach

Before leaving, Jonas asked about the "history" between Talaris and Azerach.

The Choir Bloom Explained:

  • Before the Usurpation, they were colleagues
  • Talaris was a physician; Azerach was a confessor
  • Both served the Maker
  • When the Thirteen conspired to murder their god, these two were close
  • Talaris took the Liver (regeneration, the cycle); Azerach took the Ears (secrets, truths)
  • After the Usurpation, they disagreed: Azerach hoards, Talaris cycles
  • They haven't spoken in 800 years
  • Jonas carries Azerach's brand through Talaris's body — "a message he did not ask to receive"
"Do you know, fire-bringer? What message do you carry?"

Jonas: "I'm not exactly carrying any message."

The Choir Bloom accepted this as truth — but noted that Azerach doesn't mark without purpose. The message may exist even if Jonas doesn't know it.

Verdant Pass — The Wound

The party continued past the Grove Wardens — thirty-foot humanoids of wood and bark who bowed their heads as the party passed.

The forest changed. Bioluminescence dimmed. Undergrowth thinned. The sounds of feeding faded.

The Threshold: A line in the earth. On one side: the Blooming Rot — moss, fungi, roots, life consuming death. On the other: nothing. Grey stone. Bare rock. A narrow ravine where the forest refuses to grow.

The Wound was made when Talaris claimed this territory. Something resisted. Something cut him. The cut healed around the resistance but never consumed it.

Inside the Wound:

  • Cold, dry air
  • Absolute silence — no insects, no birdsong, no wind
  • The Bleeding Blade went cold and quiet — no life to drink
  • The shard of Aedwynn in Sol pulsed with recognition

The Cave of Waiting

A darkness in the stone. A mouth in the cliff face. Inside: a small chamber, twenty feet across.

And in the center, on bare stone that has never known decay:

ELIAS ALETHAIN

A skeleton. Human. Male. Lying on his back, hands folded across his chest. Twenty-two years. No rot. No fungal growth. No reclamation. The bones are clean. White. Pristine.

He'd been waiting.

The Reunion

Fursen laid Mira's bones beside Elias.

He lowered the barrel. Opened it. Lifted out Mira's remains — the skull, the ribcage, the spine, the arms that swung Oathkeeper, the hands that held Elias on their wedding night.

He laid them beside the other skeleton. Parallel. Close enough to touch.

Two skeletons on bare stone. Fifty-two years of separation. Finally together.

Sol placed the wedding ring on Mira's finger.

The ring had been cold since he took it from the gore in the Catacomb Reliquary. Cold through the Blooming Rot.

When he slid it onto her finger, it warmed. Not glowing. Just... warm. As if it remembered being worn.

Sol moved Mira's hand until her fingers rested against Elias's. The wedding ring gleamed between them.

Fursen spoke:

🦁 "I am sorry we could not reunite you in life. But at least in death, we have brought you together."

Departure

The party left the Cave of Waiting. The two skeletons remained — holding hands in a place where nothing rots. Husband and wife. Healer and saint.

The ravine opened ahead. The party emerged from Verdant Pass into rolling hills, brown grass, normal trees. The first true sunrise they'd seen in days.

They were out of the Blooming Rot.

---

SESSION 19 — COMPLETE

Summary

Quest Completed:

  • Mira Alethain's remains reunited with Elias Alethain at the Cave of Waiting
  • Wedding ring placed on her finger
  • Fifty-two years of separation ended
Major Story Events:
  • Sol shared Aedwynn's memory with Talaris Bloomrend through the spore network
  • Talaris saw the Maker alive for the first time since the Usurpation
  • The party learned Talaris and Azerach were colleagues before becoming Sovereigns
  • They haven't spoken in 800 years
  • Jonas carries Azerach's mark through Talaris's domain — significance unknown
Combat Encounters:
  • Bloom Hounds x5 (burned)
Consequences:
  • The forest marked the party as "fire-bringers"
  • Jonas lied to a Choir Bloom (detected)
  • Sol's offering of Aedwynn's memory bought passage and softened the fire offense
  • Talaris is now "curious" about Jonas
Revelations — Ledger of Moments:
  • Four other Leonin remain in Imhuran's temporal cells
  • Ultharion the Obsidian Shepherd is asking about Leonin historical records

Experience Gained

SourceXP
Sweetwater Marsh Navigation500
Hunger Garden Survival1,000
Congregation Grove Questioning1,500
Sol's Offering (Major Story Moment)2,500
Completing Mira's Quest3,000
Total19,200
Per Character (÷3)6,400

Current Status

Party Location: Beyond Verdant Pass (exited the Blooming Rot) Time: Morning, Day 3

Party Members:
CharacterStatusNotes
JonasActiveBleeding Blade sated; Azerach's brand noted by Talaris
Sol RavenActiveShared Aedwynn's memory; wedding ring delivered
FursenActiveQuest complete; barrel empty

Outstanding Threats

ThreatStatus
The ConfluenceHAS OCCURRED (Day 3) — consequences unknown
The CardinalActive, identity unknown
UltharionKnows Savaros is dead; asking about Leonin
The MisrememberedWill return (weeks/months)
Seraphine ValeIn Veinspire, faith shattered, cover fragile

Critical Note — The Confluence

The Confluence was scheduled for Day 3 (new moon). While the party was laying Mira to rest in Verdant Pass, the ritual occurred in Veinspire.

The party has no information about what happened.

Seraphine Vale is their only contact in the city. Whatever the Cardinal planned — whatever was supposed to shift the hierarchy and change everything "irreversibly" — has already happened.

---

Related

  • Mira Alethain
  • Elias Alethain
  • Talaris Bloomrend the Verdant King
  • The Blooming Rot
  • Verdant Pass
  • The Confluence
  • Azerach the Whispered King
  • The Bleeding Blade

items.title: Coagulated Guardians
items.content:
Coagulated Guardians

---

Overview

Danger
The Blood That Watches
Type: Aberration
Origin: Mareatha the Vein-Mother's arterial passages
Role: Patrol units, containment response
First Encountered: Session 18 (Arterial Passage to Catacomb Reliquary)

---

Quick Reference

AttributeValue
SizeMedium
TypeAberration
AlignmentNeutral Evil
HabitatArterial passages, sacred Mareathan sites
IntelligenceLow (follows protocols, queries for authorization)
---

Description

Coagulated Guardians are living security systems formed from Mareatha's divine blood. They have no permanent form — they exist as part of the arterial walls until intruders are detected, then congeal into humanoid shapes to investigate or contain.

Physical Appearance:

  • Roughly humanoid shape
  • Made entirely of thickened, clotted blood
  • Faceless — smooth ovoid where head should be
  • Too-long limbs that bend without bones
  • Surface ripples with tiny currents
  • Six or seven fingers per hand
Behavior:
  • Patrol through vessel walls (blood is their medium)
  • Respond to Tribunal sigils
  • Query unrecognized bearers for authorization
  • Can split from one large form into multiple smaller forms
  • Communicate through skull-vibration (telepathic speech)
---

Communication Protocol

When encountering intruders, guardians follow a verification sequence:

1. Manifest — Congeal from wall to investigate 2. Scan — Sense blood, divine presence, sigils 3. Query — Request authorization if sigil recognized but bearer unknown 4. Respond — Accept authorization or initiate containment

Query Format:

⟨ SIGIL: [STATUS] ⟩
⟨ BEARER: [STATUS] ⟩
⟨ SPECIES: [STATUS/ERROR] ⟩
⟨ QUERY: AUTHORIZATION SOURCE? ⟩

Note: Leonin register as [ERROR] — likely due to Imhuran's erasure of their species from official records.

---

Tactical Notes

Vulnerabilities to Exploit:

  • Fire damage is doubled — extremely effective
  • Cold damage is doubled — freezes the blood solid
  • Cannot pursue through sealed arterial valves
  • Low intelligence — follows protocols rigidly
Dangers:
  • Blood Sense makes hiding nearly impossible
  • Wall Merger provides total cover and ambush capability
  • Can split when "killed" — prepare for reinforcements
  • Engulf can quickly incapacitate isolated targets
  • Patrol patterns mean multiple guardians may respond
Authorization Bypass:
  • Tribunal sigils are recognized automatically
  • Bearer must provide valid authorization name
  • Names of dead operatives may still work (briefly)
  • High-ranking names (Savaros, The Cardinal) carry risk of verification
---

Encounter History

Session 18 — Arterial Passage

Situation: Party used dead handler's Tribunal sigil to attempt passage.

Result: Sigil recognized, but bearer (Fursen/Leonin) flagged as ERROR. Fursen failed Deception check (8) when asked for authorization source. Guardian initiated containment — split into three forms.

Combat: Party destroyed all three guardians in one round:

  • Mira bisected Guardian A
  • Sol's Firebolt exploited fire vulnerability on Guardian B
  • Fursen finished Guardian B with Flurry of Blows
  • Jonas destroyed Guardian C with Booming Blade hammer throw
Aftermath: Party sprinted to Reliquary, pursued by reinforcements until arterial valve sealed.

---

Related

  • Mareatha the Vein-Mother
  • Catacomb Reliquary
  • Savaros
  • Arterial Tunnels
  • Pulse-Wardens
---

"They don't chase. They don't need to. The blood is everywhere. The blood sees everything." — Mira Alethain

items.title: Grief-Moths
items.content:
Grief-Moths

Type: Aberration (Fey-touched) Domain Association: Eredain the Choirless Monarch Habitat: Floating Archipelago of Elystra, areas of intense emotion, mass mourning sites

---

Description

Grief-Moths are ethereal insects drawn to emotional resonance—specifically sorrow. They appear as luminous moths with wingspans of two to three feet, their wings displaying patterns that shift to reflect the grief they've consumed. Where they gather, despair deepens.

---

Appearance

Portrait
Physical Description
A moth with gossamer wings that shimmer between pale blue and bruise-purple. The wing patterns aren't static—they display flickering images: faces of the dead, moments of loss, symbols of mourning that differ for each viewer. The body is translucent, revealing a core of condensed grey light that pulses like a slow heartbeat. Its antennae are unnaturally long, constantly tasting the emotional currents of the air.

[!ai-prompt] Image Generation
Ethereal moth with large gossamer wings showing ghostly faces and mourning symbols in shifting patterns, translucent body with grey pulsing light core, extremely long feathery antennae, wingspan three feet, floating in dim atmosphere with emotional resonance waves visible, pale blue and purple coloring, dark fantasy concept art, cosmic horror aesthetic, muted colors with divine lighting accents, body horror elements, inspired by Berserk and Bloodborne, digital painting, detailed textures, ominous atmosphere, 4k

---

Behavior

Grief-Moths don't cause sorrow—they amplify it. They're drawn to funerals, battlefields, places where people weep. Their presence intensifies existing grief until it becomes debilitating.

Feeding: They consume emotional resonance. A Grief-Moth that feeds on profound loss grows brighter, its wings displaying more detailed imagery. Starving moths are nearly invisible.

Attraction: The moths communicate grief across distances. When one finds a source of sorrow, others arrive within hours. Swarms of hundreds can form around mass tragedies.

Symbiosis: Some Eredain cultists cultivate them, believing concentrated grief creates pure emotional resonance. They're used in rituals to strip away emotional defenses.

---

Combat

---

Variants

---

Ecology

Diet: Pure emotional resonance. They prefer grief but can subsist on any intense negative emotion.

Reproduction: When a grief-moth feeds on enough sorrow, it splits into two smaller moths. Neither is the "original"—both carry fragments of accumulated grief.

Lifespan: Theoretically immortal if fed. Unfed moths fade into nothing within weeks.

Natural Role: Some scholars argue they serve an ecological purpose—processing emotional residue that would otherwise poison an area. Haunted locations often lack grief-moths because they've already been "cleaned."

---

Encounter Ideas

  • A funeral procession is interrupted by a descending cloud of grief-moths—someone must protect the mourners while they complete the rites
  • The party finds a village in catatonic despair, dozens of grief-moths having turned a single death into community-wide paralysis
  • A noble offers payment to harvest grief-moth wings for a ritual—they claim it will help them "let go" of their dead spouse
---

Related

  • Eredain the Choirless Monarch
  • Monster Ecologies
---

"They don't make you sad. They show you what you've already lost. They just make sure you can't look away."

items.title: Silence-Ticks
items.content:
Silence-Ticks

Type: Aberration (Vermin) Domain Association: Azerach the Whispered King Habitat: Dominion of Still Air, areas of enforced silence, near Azerach's followers

---

Description

Silence-Ticks are thumb-sized parasites native to Azerach's domain. They appear as pale, fleshy ticks with tiny malformed ears covering their bodies instead of eyes. Where they feed, sound dies.

---

Appearance

Portrait
Physical Description
A bulbous, cream-colored tick roughly two inches across. Its body is covered in dozens of tiny ears of varying sizes—human, animal, and shapes that don't correspond to any known creature. It has no visible eyes or mouth. When it feeds, its body pulses silently, growing more translucent until you can see the stolen sounds churning inside as grey vapor.

[!ai-prompt] Image Generation
Fleshy pale tick covered in tiny malformed ears instead of eyes, translucent body showing grey vapor inside, no mouth or eyes visible, sitting on stone surface, disturbing body horror creature, dark fantasy concept art, cosmic horror aesthetic, muted colors with divine lighting accents, body horror elements, inspired by Berserk and Bloodborne, digital painting, detailed textures, ominous atmosphere, 4k

---

Behavior

Silence-Ticks don't drink blood—they consume sound. They attach to victims and create zones of absolute silence around them. The tick grows fat on stolen noise: conversations, screams, footsteps, even heartbeats.

Feeding: A single tick can drain all sound within a 10-foot radius. Victims often don't notice until they try to speak and hear nothing.

Colony Behavior: Silence-Ticks cluster together. A large enough colony can silence an entire building. Azerach's temples are infested with them intentionally.

Communication: Ticks communicate through vibration patterns undetectable to most creatures. They coordinate feeding and warn each other of threats through the ground.

---

Combat

---

Variants

---

Ecology

Diet: Sound. Starving ticks become desperate and swarm anything making noise.

Reproduction: Queens lay eggs in absolute silence. The eggs absorb ambient sound to gestate—a reason Azerach's followers deliberately speak near tick nests.

Natural Enemies: Creatures from Eredain's domain hunt them—the Choirless Monarch's servants despise anything that consumes sound.

Habitat: Anywhere Azerach's influence touches. Wild populations exist in caves and ruins where silence is natural.

---

Encounter Ideas

  • Party enters an area and realizes they can't hear each other speak—ticks are attached to their gear
  • A village is slowly going silent as a tick colony spreads through the walls
  • Azerach's cultists use tick-infested cloaks to move without sound
---

Related

  • Azerach the Whispered King
  • Monster Ecologies
  • Sibilants
---

"The worst part isn't the silence. It's when you kill one and hear everything it ate—every scream, every plea, every prayer—all at once."

items.title: The Misremembered
items.content:
The Misremembered

Classification: Conceptual Predator / Ontological Correction Domain Association: Karthayne the Hollow Crown (creation, destruction, authority) First Encounter: Session 8A — The Span That Watches Major Battle: Session 16 — The Wrong Thought Status: Driven off, will return

---

ORIGIN

The Misremembered is a function of Karthayne the Hollow Crown—the Sovereign who holds the Maker's Heart of Creation.

When Karthayne claimed the Heart of Creation, he lost his memories—his identity scoured away by an organ meant for a god. To compensate, he began compiling The Cosmic Census: a complete record of every existing thing in the world. If he cannot remember himself, he will remember everything else.

But records reveal gaps. Anomalies. Things that exist but aren't documented. Things that shouldn't exist.

The Misremembered was created to hunt these anomalies. It is Karthayne's correction tool—sent to find things that don't fit the records and erase them from existence. Not kill them. Erase them. As if they never were.

The Misremembered hunts "errors in reality"—beings and concepts that don't fit the pattern of creation Karthayne inherited from Aedwynn. They are corrections. Functions. Tools of a creative authority that cannot tolerate flaws.

---

CONNECTION TO THE THRONE VOID

The Misremembered retreated to The Throne Void—Karthayne's hidden domain, located off the known map of the Ashen Realms. This is where:

  • Karthayne maintains his Cosmic Census
  • The archives of all existence are kept
  • Anomalies are processed and erased
  • The Misremembered returns to report and regenerate
The Throne Void is where broken and impossible things go—either to be catalogued or unmade.

---

APPEARANCE

Primary Form:

  • Tall, too tall for normal humanoid proportions
  • Can perfectly mimic living beings (with subtle flaws)
  • Head tilts at predatory angles—suggests broken neck
  • Hollow black spaces where eyes should be
  • Shadows behave incorrectly (turn opposite way, move independently)
  • Can fold like paper and slip through impossible spaces
Voice:
  • Layered whispers
  • Like pages sliding over each other
  • Sound of thought without a mouth
  • Breath escaping a closed book
  • When mimicking, uses stolen voices with underlying discordant whispers
---

ABILITIES

Form Mimicry:

  • Can create perfect duplicates of living beings
  • Small flaws always present (wrong shadow direction, too-sharp outlines, hollow eyes)
  • Duplicates are conceptual rather than physical
Reality Manipulation:
  • Can freeze objects mid-motion
  • Moves by "jumping" closer without physically crossing space
  • Exists partially outside normal physics
  • Leaves messages carved/written in materials without tools
Conceptual Form:
  • Made of stolen memories and wrong thoughts
  • Resistant to physical damage
  • Can fold along angles that don't exist in three-dimensional space
  • Regenerates unless disrupted by significant force
Correction Touch:
  • Can attempt to unmake beings from existence
  • Victims don't die—they are erased, as if they never existed
  • Those nearby forget the victim existed
Tracking:
  • Hunts conceptual anomalies and ontological errors
  • Can sense "wrong thoughts"—beings that shouldn't exist
  • Patient and methodical, not aggressive
  • Tests boundaries before striking
---

Medium aberration, neutral evil

Armor Class: 16 (unnatural geometry)

STRDEXCONINTWISCHA
14 (+2)18 (+4)18 (+4)20 (+5)16 (+3)18 (+4)
Saving Throws: Dex +8, Int +9, Wis +7, Cha +8 Skills: Deception +8, Insight +7, Perception +7, Stealth +8 Damage Vulnerabilities: Radiant Senses: Truesight 120 ft., passive Perception 17 Languages: All languages (speaks in stolen voices)

Traits

Conceptual Form. Advantage on saves against form alteration, banishment, or thought detection.

Impossible Step. Can teleport up to 15 feet as part of movement. Doesn't provoke opportunity attacks.

Stolen Shape. Can perfectly mimic any observed creature. DC 18 Perception to spot flaws.

Regeneration. Regains 10 HP at start of turn. Radiant damage prevents this for one turn.

Aura of Unmaking (Recharge 5-6). Creatures within 10 feet: DC 16 Wisdom save or 14 (4d6) psychic damage and disadvantage on attacks until end of next turn.

Reactions

Fold Away. When hit by attack, gains resistance to that damage.

TARGET: SOL RAVEN

Why Sol?

  • Sol is connected to Aedwynn the Maker—the god who died before Karthayne claimed the Heart
  • Sol possesses the Resonant Codex, which now contains Kael's echo—a dead paradox living inside an artifact
  • Karthayne cannot fully process Aedwynn's legacy—it predates his authority
  • Sol registers as a "wrong thought"—a concept that exists outside proper creation
  • To the Heart of Creation, Sol is a flaw that must be corrected
The Memory Coin Trigger: During the 13-Hour Trial in Imhuran's domain, the party acquired a memory coin—an item that records and triggers remembrance. When the Misremembered encountered Sol while he carried this coin, the artifact forced a connection.

The coin made the Misremembered remember Sol. And remembering meant recognizing him as an anomaly. Recognizing meant targeting for erasure.

Without the coin, Sol might have remained unnoticed—just another mortal beneath the attention of cosmic correction. The coin changed everything.

What It Wants: The Misremembered doesn't want to kill Sol. It wants to correct him—unmake him from existence so that he never was. This is worse than death.

Current Status: Sol is flagged in Karthayne's records as an anomaly, but he is considered insignificant. A mortal. A speck. The Misremembered was deemed sufficient to handle him—and it failed.

Karthayne has not escalated. Sol is too unimportant for the Sovereign's personal attention.

Sol's best protection is being beneath notice. The moment he becomes important—does something significant, gains real power, threatens the cosmic order—Karthayne might actually look his way.

Kael's Status: Kael was also flagged as an anomaly (a paradox vomited out by Vor'Kael the Hollow Empress). However, Kael is now dead. Case closed from Karthayne's perspective.

But—Kael's echo lives in Sol's Resonant Codex. This potentially makes Sol a bigger anomaly now. The records have not yet been updated to reflect this development.

---

ENCOUNTER HISTORY

Session 8A — The Span That Watches

1. Appeared in stables where Fursen slept 2. Mimicked Fursen to test and observe 3. Asked about Sol's location: "Where is the wrong thought?" 4. Party tracked it to settlement boundary 5. Fursen gave it a memory coin (acquired during the 13-Hour Trial) 6. Entity consumed the coin and became stronger/more coherent—the coin triggered its ability to remember 7. Identified Sol: "You. You are the wrong thought." 8. Left message: "FOUND YOU" 9. Departed without attacking

Note: The memory coin was the catalyst. Without it, the Misremembered might never have recognized Sol as an anomaly. The party inadvertently painted a target on Sol's back.

Session 16 — The Wrong Thought

1. Manifested at Pallid Crossroads during party's rest 2. Settlement went wrong—mist surrounded area, people vanished 3. Appeared wearing Sol's shape (with flaws) 4. Explained its nature and purpose to Sol 5. Jonas intervened—threw hammer at its hand (rolled 24, hit) 6. Hand crumpled "like wet paper," dripped black ink 7. Full combat ensued 8. Party defeated it through combined assault 9. Entity folded inward along impossible angles and vanished 10. Left note: "NOT CORRECTED. NOTED. WILL RETURN."

---

BEHAVIOR

Hunting Pattern:

  • Observes before acting
  • Tests prey's reactions and defenses
  • Makes its presence known deliberately
  • Uses psychological pressure over physical force
  • Communicates through cryptic statements
  • Patient—will wait weeks or months between attempts
Known Statements:
  • "Not you." (to Fursen)
  • "Where is the wrong thought?" (seeking Sol)
  • "...Memory... returns..." (after consuming lifespan coin)
  • "You. You are the wrong thought." (to Sol)
  • "The Empress remembers everything... But she doesn't remember you."
  • "You're a thought that thinks itself without permission."
  • "NOT CORRECTED. NOTED. WILL RETURN."
---

CURRENT STATUS

Condition: Disrupted, reforming Location: Unknown—folded out of physical space Timeline: Will return in weeks or months Threat Level: EXTREME

The party hurt it. This is rare. This means they can hurt it again.

But it cannot be killed by conventional means. It was disrupted, not destroyed. It will reconstitute and return.

---

PARTY KNOWLEDGE

The party now knows:

  • Its name (The Misremembered)
  • Its origin (Karthayne the Hollow Crown)
  • Its purpose (correcting errors in creation)
  • Why it hunts Sol (connection to Aedwynn)
  • It can be hurt (Jonas's hammer worked)
  • It will return (the note confirms this)
  • Radiant damage is effective against it
---

Related

  • Sol Raven
  • Karthayne the Hollow Crown
  • Aedwynn the Maker
  • Campaign Sessions

**Major Battle:** Session 16 — The Wrong Thought
**Status:** Driven off, will return

---

## ORIGIN

The Misremembered is a function of [[Karthayne the Hollow Crown]]—the Sovereign who holds the Maker's Heart of Creation.
